76/* Forward declarations needed by redismodule.h and keymeta.h */
77struct redisObject;
78struct RedisModule;
79
80/* This is a structure used to export some meta-information such as dbid to the module. */
81struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx {
82 struct redisObject *from_key, *to_key; /* Optional name of key processed, NULL when unknown.
83 In most cases, only 'from_key' is valid, but in callbacks
84 such as `copy2`, both 'from_key' and 'to_key' are valid. */
85 int from_dbid, to_dbid; /* The dbid of the key being processed, -1 when unknown.
86 In most cases, only 'from_dbid' is valid, but in callbacks such
87 as `copy2`, 'from_dbid' and 'to_dbid' are both valid. */
88};

104/* helpers */
105#define numElements(x) (sizeof(x)/sizeof((x)[0]))
106
107/* min/max */
108#undef min
109#undef max
110#define min(a, b) ((a) < (b) ? (a) : (b))
111#define max(a, b) ((a) > (b) ? (a) : (b))
112
113/* Get the pointer of the outer struct from a member address */
114#define redis_member2struct(struct_name, member_name, member_addr) \
115 ((struct_name *)((char*)member_addr - offsetof(struct_name, member_name)))
116
117/* Error codes */
118#define C_OK 0
119#define C_ERR -1
120#define C_RETRY -2

122/* Static server configuration */
123#define CONFIG_DEFAULT_HZ 10 /* Time interrupt calls/sec. */
124#define CONFIG_MIN_HZ 1
125#define CONFIG_MAX_HZ 500
126#define MAX_CLIENTS_PER_CLOCK_TICK 200 /* HZ is adapted based on that. */
127#define CRON_DBS_PER_CALL 16
128#define CRON_DICTS_PER_DB 16
129#define NET_MAX_WRITES_PER_EVENT (1024*64)
130#define PROTO_SHARED_SELECT_CMDS 10
131#define OBJ_SHARED_INTEGERS 10000
132#define OBJ_SHARED_BULKHDR_LEN 32
133#define OBJ_SHARED_HDR_STRLEN(_len_) (((_len_) < 10) ? 4 : 5) /* see shared.mbulkhdr etc. */
134#define LOG_MAX_LEN 1024 /* Default maximum length of syslog messages.*/
135#define AOF_REWRITE_ITEMS_PER_CMD 64
136#define AOF_ANNOTATION_LINE_MAX_LEN 1024
137#define CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE 40
138#define RDB_EOF_MARK_SIZE 40
139#define CONFIG_REPL_BACKLOG_MIN_SIZE (1024*16) /* 16k */
140#define CONFIG_BGSAVE_RETRY_DELAY 5 /* Wait a few secs before trying again. */
141#define CONFIG_DEFAULT_PID_FILE "/var/run/redis.pid"
142#define CONFIG_DEFAULT_BINDADDR_COUNT 2
143#define CONFIG_DEFAULT_BINDADDR { "*", "-::*" }
144#define NET_HOST_STR_LEN 256 /* Longest valid hostname */
145#define NET_IP_STR_LEN 46 /* INET6_ADDRSTRLEN is 46, but we need to be sure */
146#define NET_ADDR_STR_LEN (NET_IP_STR_LEN+32) /* Must be enough for ip:port */
147#define NET_HOST_PORT_STR_LEN (NET_HOST_STR_LEN+32) /* Must be enough for hostname:port */
148#define CONFIG_BINDADDR_MAX 16
149#define CONFIG_MIN_RESERVED_FDS 32
150#define CONFIG_DEFAULT_PROC_TITLE_TEMPLATE "{title} {listen-addr} {server-mode}"
151#define INCREMENTAL_REHASHING_THRESHOLD_US 1000
152#define CLIENTS_CRON_MIN_ITERATIONS 5
153
154/* Stream IDMP configuration limits */
155#define CONFIG_STREAM_IDMP_MIN_DURATION 1 /* Min IDMP duration in seconds. */
156#define CONFIG_STREAM_IDMP_MAX_DURATION 86400 /* Max IDMP duration in seconds (24 hours). */
157#define CONFIG_STREAM_IDMP_MIN_MAXSIZE 1 /* Min IDMP max entries. */
158#define CONFIG_STREAM_IDMP_MAX_MAXSIZE 10000 /* Max IDMP max entries. */
159
160/* Bucket sizes for client eviction pools. Each bucket stores clients with
161 * memory usage of up to twice the size of the bucket below it. */
162#define CLIENT_MEM_USAGE_BUCKET_MIN_LOG 15 /* Bucket sizes start at up to 32KB (2^15) */
163#define CLIENT_MEM_USAGE_BUCKET_MAX_LOG 33 /* Bucket for largest clients: sizes above 4GB (2^32) */
164#define CLIENT_MEM_USAGE_BUCKETS (1+CLIENT_MEM_USAGE_BUCKET_MAX_LOG-CLIENT_MEM_USAGE_BUCKET_MIN_LOG)
165
166#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_SLOW 0
167#define ACTIVE_EXPIRE_CYCLE_FAST 1
168
169/* Children process will exit with this status code to signal that the
170 * process terminated without an error: this is useful in order to kill
171 * a saving child (RDB or AOF one), without triggering in the parent the
172 * write protection that is normally turned on on write errors.
173 * Usually children that are terminated with SIGUSR1 will exit with this
174 * special code. */
175#define SERVER_CHILD_NOERROR_RETVAL 255
176
177/* Reading copy-on-write info is sometimes expensive and may slow down child
178 * processes that report it continuously. We measure the cost of obtaining it
179 * and hold back additional reading based on this factor. */
180#define CHILD_COW_DUTY_CYCLE 100
181
182/* Instantaneous metrics tracking. */
183#define STATS_METRIC_SAMPLES 16 /* Number of samples per metric. */
184#define STATS_METRIC_COMMAND 0 /* Number of commands executed. */
185#define STATS_METRIC_NET_INPUT 1 /* Bytes read from network. */
186#define STATS_METRIC_NET_OUTPUT 2 /* Bytes written to network. */
187#define STATS_METRIC_NET_INPUT_REPLICATION 3 /* Bytes read from network during replication. */
188#define STATS_METRIC_NET_OUTPUT_REPLICATION 4 /* Bytes written to network during replication. */
189#define STATS_METRIC_EL_CYCLE 5 /* Number of eventloop cycled. */
190#define STATS_METRIC_EL_DURATION 6 /* Eventloop duration. */
191#define STATS_METRIC_COUNT 7
192
193/* Protocol and I/O related defines */
194#define PROTO_IOBUF_LEN (1024*16) /* Generic I/O buffer size */
195#define PROTO_REPLY_CHUNK_BYTES (16*1024) /* 16k output buffer */
196#define PROTO_INLINE_MAX_SIZE (1024*64) /* Max size of inline reads */
197#define PROTO_MBULK_BIG_ARG (1024*32)
198#define PROTO_RESIZE_THRESHOLD (1024*32) /* Threshold for determining whether to resize query buffer */
199#define PROTO_REPLY_MIN_BYTES (1024) /* the lower limit on reply buffer size */
200#define REDIS_AUTOSYNC_BYTES (1024*1024*4) /* Sync file every 4MB. */
201
202#define REPLY_BUFFER_DEFAULT_PEAK_RESET_TIME 5000 /* 5 seconds */
203
204/* Reply copy avoidance thresholds */
205#define COPY_AVOID_MIN_IO_THREADS 7 /* Minimum number of IO threads for copy avoidance */
206#define COPY_AVOID_MIN_STRING_SIZE 16384 /* Minimum bulk string size for copy avoidance (no IO threads) */
207#define COPY_AVOID_MIN_STRING_SIZE_THREADED 65536 /* Minimum bulk string size for copy avoidance (with IO threads) */
208
209/* When configuring the server eventloop, we setup it so that the total number
210 * of file descriptors we can handle are server.maxclients + RESERVED_FDS +
211 * a few more to stay safe. Since RESERVED_FDS defaults to 32, we add 96
212 * in order to make sure of not over provisioning more than 128 fds. */
213#define CONFIG_FDSET_INCR (CONFIG_MIN_RESERVED_FDS+96)
214
215/* Default lookahead value */
216#define REDIS_DEFAULT_LOOKAHEAD 16
217
218/* OOM Score Adjustment classes. */
219#define CONFIG_OOM_MASTER 0
220#define CONFIG_OOM_REPLICA 1
221#define CONFIG_OOM_BGCHILD 2
222#define CONFIG_OOM_COUNT 3
223
224extern int configOOMScoreAdjValuesDefaults[CONFIG_OOM_COUNT];
225
226/* Hash table parameters */
227#define HASHTABLE_MAX_LOAD_FACTOR 1.618 /* Maximum hash table load factor. */
228
229/* Max number of IO threads */
230#define IO_THREADS_MAX_NUM 128
231
232/* To make IO threads and main thread run in parallel, we will transfer clients
233 * between them if the number of clients in the pending list reaches this value. */
234#define IO_THREAD_MAX_PENDING_CLIENTS 16
235
236/* Main thread id for doing IO work, whatever we enable or disable io thread
237 * the main thread always does IO work, so we can consider that the main thread
238 * is the io thread 0. */
239#define IOTHREAD_MAIN_THREAD_ID 0
240
241/* Command flags. Please check the definition of struct redisCommand in this file
242 * for more information about the meaning of every flag. */
243#define CMD_WRITE (1ULL<<0)
244#define CMD_READONLY (1ULL<<1)
245#define CMD_DENYOOM (1ULL<<2)
246#define CMD_MODULE (1ULL<<3) /* Command exported by module. */
247#define CMD_ADMIN (1ULL<<4)
248#define CMD_PUBSUB (1ULL<<5)
249#define CMD_NOSCRIPT (1ULL<<6)
250#define CMD_BLOCKING (1ULL<<8) /* Has potential to block. */
251#define CMD_LOADING (1ULL<<9)
252#define CMD_STALE (1ULL<<10)
253#define CMD_SKIP_MONITOR (1ULL<<11)
254#define CMD_SKIP_SLOWLOG (1ULL<<12)
255#define CMD_ASKING (1ULL<<13)
256#define CMD_FAST (1ULL<<14)
257#define CMD_NO_AUTH (1ULL<<15)
258#define CMD_MAY_REPLICATE (1ULL<<16)
259#define CMD_SENTINEL (1ULL<<17)
260#define CMD_ONLY_SENTINEL (1ULL<<18)
261#define CMD_NO_MANDATORY_KEYS (1ULL<<19)
262#define CMD_PROTECTED (1ULL<<20)
263#define CMD_MODULE_GETKEYS (1ULL<<21) /* Use the modules getkeys interface. */
264#define CMD_MODULE_NO_CLUSTER (1ULL<<22) /* Deny on Redis Cluster. */
265#define CMD_NO_ASYNC_LOADING (1ULL<<23)
266#define CMD_NO_MULTI (1ULL<<24)
267#define CMD_MOVABLE_KEYS (1ULL<<25) /* The legacy range spec doesn't cover all keys.
268 * Populated by populateCommandLegacyRangeSpec. */
269#define CMD_ALLOW_BUSY ((1ULL<<26))
270#define CMD_MODULE_GETCHANNELS (1ULL<<27) /* Use the modules getchannels interface. */
271#define CMD_TOUCHES_ARBITRARY_KEYS (1ULL<<28)
272#define CMD_INTERNAL (1ULL<<29) /* Internal command. */
273
274/* Command flags that describe ACLs categories. */
275#define ACL_CATEGORY_KEYSPACE (1ULL<<0)
276#define ACL_CATEGORY_READ (1ULL<<1)
277#define ACL_CATEGORY_WRITE (1ULL<<2)
278#define ACL_CATEGORY_SET (1ULL<<3)
279#define ACL_CATEGORY_SORTEDSET (1ULL<<4)
280#define ACL_CATEGORY_LIST (1ULL<<5)
281#define ACL_CATEGORY_HASH (1ULL<<6)
282#define ACL_CATEGORY_STRING (1ULL<<7)
283#define ACL_CATEGORY_BITMAP (1ULL<<8)
284#define ACL_CATEGORY_HYPERLOGLOG (1ULL<<9)
285#define ACL_CATEGORY_GEO (1ULL<<10)
286#define ACL_CATEGORY_STREAM (1ULL<<11)
287#define ACL_CATEGORY_PUBSUB (1ULL<<12)
288#define ACL_CATEGORY_ADMIN (1ULL<<13)
289#define ACL_CATEGORY_FAST (1ULL<<14)
290#define ACL_CATEGORY_SLOW (1ULL<<15)
291#define ACL_CATEGORY_BLOCKING (1ULL<<16)
292#define ACL_CATEGORY_DANGEROUS (1ULL<<17)
293#define ACL_CATEGORY_CONNECTION (1ULL<<18)
294#define ACL_CATEGORY_TRANSACTION (1ULL<<19)
295#define ACL_CATEGORY_SCRIPTING (1ULL<<20)
296
297/* Key-spec flags *
298 * -------------- */
299/* The following refer what the command actually does with the value or metadata
300 * of the key, and not necessarily the user data or how it affects it.
301 * Each key-spec may must have exactly one of these. Any operation that's not
302 * distinctly deletion, overwrite or read-only would be marked as RW. */
303#define CMD_KEY_RO (1ULL<<0) /* Read-Only - Reads the value of the key, but
304 * doesn't necessarily returns it. */
305#define CMD_KEY_RW (1ULL<<1) /* Read-Write - Reads and modifies/deletes
306 * the data stored in the value of the key or
307 * its metadata. */
308#define CMD_KEY_OW (1ULL<<2) /* Overwrite - Overwrites the data stored in
309 * the value of the key. */
310#define CMD_KEY_RM (1ULL<<3) /* Deletes the key without reading it's value. */
311/* The following refer to user data inside the value of the key, not the metadata
312 * like LRU, type, cardinality. It refers to the logical operation on the user's
313 * data (actual input strings / TTL), being used / returned / copied / changed,
314 * It doesn't refer to modification or returning of metadata (like type, count,
315 * presence of data). Any write that's not INSERT or DELETE, would be an UPDATE.
316 * Each key-spec may have one of the writes with or without access, or none: */
317#define CMD_KEY_ACCESS (1ULL<<4) /* Returns, copies or uses the user data from
318 * the value of the key. */
319#define CMD_KEY_UPDATE (1ULL<<5) /* Updates data to the value, new value may
320 * depend on the old value. */
321#define CMD_KEY_INSERT (1ULL<<6) /* Adds data to the value with no chance of
322 * modification or deletion of existing data. */
323#define CMD_KEY_DELETE (1ULL<<7) /* Explicitly deletes some content
324 * from the value of the key. */
325/* Other flags: */
326#define CMD_KEY_NOT_KEY (1ULL<<8) /* A 'fake' key that should be routed
327 * like a key in cluster mode but is
328 * excluded from other key checks. */
329#define CMD_KEY_INCOMPLETE (1ULL<<9) /* Means that the keyspec might not point
330 * out to all keys it should cover */
331#define CMD_KEY_VARIABLE_FLAGS (1ULL<<10) /* Means that some keys might have
332 * different flags depending on arguments */
333#define CMD_KEY_PREFIX (1ULL<<11) /* Given key represents a prefix of a set of keys */
334
335/* Key flags for when access type is unknown */
336#define CMD_KEY_FULL_ACCESS (CMD_KEY_RW | CMD_KEY_ACCESS | CMD_KEY_UPDATE)
337
338/* Key flags for how key is removed */
339#define DB_FLAG_KEY_NONE 0
340#define DB_FLAG_KEY_DELETED (1ULL<<0)
341#define DB_FLAG_KEY_EXPIRED (1ULL<<1)
342#define DB_FLAG_KEY_EVICTED (1ULL<<2)
343#define DB_FLAG_KEY_OVERWRITE (1ULL<<3)
344#define DB_FLAG_NO_UPDATE_KEYSIZES (1ULL<<4) /* Don't update keysizes histograms */
345
346/* Channel flags share the same flag space as the key flags */
347#define CMD_CHANNEL_PATTERN (1ULL<<11) /* The argument is a channel pattern */
348#define CMD_CHANNEL_SUBSCRIBE (1ULL<<12) /* The command subscribes to channels */
349#define CMD_CHANNEL_UNSUBSCRIBE (1ULL<<13) /* The command unsubscribes to channels */
350#define CMD_CHANNEL_PUBLISH (1ULL<<14) /* The command publishes to channels. */
351
352/* AOF states */
353#define AOF_OFF 0 /* AOF is off */
354#define AOF_ON 1 /* AOF is on */
355#define AOF_WAIT_REWRITE 2 /* AOF waits rewrite to start appending */
356
357/* AOF return values for loadAppendOnlyFiles() and loadSingleAppendOnlyFile() */
358#define AOF_OK 0
359#define AOF_NOT_EXIST 1
360#define AOF_EMPTY 2
361#define AOF_OPEN_ERR 3
362#define AOF_FAILED 4
363#define AOF_TRUNCATED 5
364#define AOF_BROKEN_RECOVERED 6
365
366/* RDB return values for rdbLoad. */
367#define RDB_OK 0
368#define RDB_NOT_EXIST 1 /* RDB file doesn't exist. */
369#define RDB_FAILED 2 /* Failed to load the RDB file. */
370
371/* Command doc flags */
372#define CMD_DOC_NONE 0
373#define CMD_DOC_DEPRECATED (1<<0) /* Command is deprecated */
374#define CMD_DOC_SYSCMD (1<<1) /* System (internal) command */
375
376/* Client flags */
377#define CLIENT_SLAVE (1<<0) /* This client is a replica */
378#define CLIENT_MASTER (1<<1) /* This client is a master */
379#define CLIENT_MONITOR (1<<2) /* This client is a slave monitor, see MONITOR */
380#define CLIENT_MULTI (1<<3) /* This client is in a MULTI context */
381#define CLIENT_BLOCKED (1<<4) /* The client is waiting in a blocking operation */
382#define CLIENT_DIRTY_CAS (1<<5) /* Watched keys modified. EXEC will fail. */
383#define CLIENT_CLOSE_AFTER_REPLY (1<<6) /* Close after writing entire reply. */
384#define CLIENT_UNBLOCKED (1<<7) /* This client was unblocked and is stored in
385 server.unblocked_clients */
386#define CLIENT_SCRIPT (1<<8) /* This is a non connected client used by Lua */
387#define CLIENT_ASKING (1<<9) /* Client issued the ASKING command */
388#define CLIENT_CLOSE_ASAP (1<<10)/* Close this client ASAP */
389#define CLIENT_UNIX_SOCKET (1<<11) /* Client connected via Unix domain socket */
390#define CLIENT_DIRTY_EXEC (1<<12) /* EXEC will fail for errors while queueing */
391#define CLIENT_MASTER_FORCE_REPLY (1<<13) /* Queue replies even if is master */
392#define CLIENT_FORCE_AOF (1<<14) /* Force AOF propagation of current cmd. */
393#define CLIENT_FORCE_REPL (1<<15) /* Force replication of current cmd. */
394#define CLIENT_PRE_PSYNC (1<<16) /* Instance don't understand PSYNC. */
395#define CLIENT_READONLY (1<<17) /* Cluster client is in read-only state. */
396#define CLIENT_PUBSUB (1<<18) /* Client is in Pub/Sub mode. */
397#define CLIENT_PREVENT_AOF_PROP (1<<19) /* Don't propagate to AOF. */
398#define CLIENT_PREVENT_REPL_PROP (1<<20) /* Don't propagate to slaves. */
399#define CLIENT_PREVENT_PROP (CLIENT_PREVENT_AOF_PROP|CLIENT_PREVENT_REPL_PROP)
400#define CLIENT_PENDING_WRITE (1<<21) /* Client has output to send but a write
401 handler is yet not installed. */
402#define CLIENT_REPLY_OFF (1<<22) /* Don't send replies to client. */
403#define CLIENT_REPLY_SKIP_NEXT (1<<23) /* Set CLIENT_REPLY_SKIP for next cmd */
404#define CLIENT_REPLY_SKIP (1<<24) /* Don't send just this reply. */
405#define CLIENT_LUA_DEBUG (1<<25) /* Run EVAL in debug mode. */
406#define CLIENT_LUA_DEBUG_SYNC (1<<26) /* EVAL debugging without fork() */
407#define CLIENT_MODULE (1<<27) /* Non connected client used by some module. */
408#define CLIENT_PROTECTED (1<<28) /* Client should not be freed for now. */
409#define CLIENT_EXECUTING_COMMAND (1<<29) /* Indicates that the client is currently in the process of handling
410 a command. usually this will be marked only during call()
411 however, blocked clients might have this flag kept until they
412 will try to reprocess the command. */
413
414#define CLIENT_PENDING_COMMAND (1<<30) /* Indicates the client has a fully
415 * parsed command ready for execution. */
416#define CLIENT_TRACKING (1ULL<<31) /* Client enabled keys tracking in order to
417 perform client side caching. */
418#define CLIENT_TRACKING_BROKEN_REDIR (1ULL<<32) /* Target client is invalid. */
419#define CLIENT_TRACKING_BCAST (1ULL<<33) /* Tracking in BCAST mode. */
420#define CLIENT_TRACKING_OPTIN (1ULL<<34) /* Tracking in opt-in mode. */
421#define CLIENT_TRACKING_OPTOUT (1ULL<<35) /* Tracking in opt-out mode. */
422#define CLIENT_TRACKING_CACHING (1ULL<<36) /* CACHING yes/no was given,
423 depending on optin/optout mode. */
424#define CLIENT_TRACKING_NOLOOP (1ULL<<37) /* Don't send invalidation messages
425 about writes performed by myself.*/
426#define CLIENT_IN_TO_TABLE (1ULL<<38) /* This client is in the timeout table. */
427#define CLIENT_PROTOCOL_ERROR (1ULL<<39) /* Protocol error chatting with it. */
428#define CLIENT_CLOSE_AFTER_COMMAND (1ULL<<40) /* Close after executing commands
429 * and writing entire reply. */
430#define CLIENT_DENY_BLOCKING (1ULL<<41) /* Indicate that the client should not be blocked.
431 currently, turned on inside MULTI, Lua, RM_Call,
432 and AOF client */
433#define CLIENT_REPL_RDBONLY (1ULL<<42) /* This client is a replica that only wants
434 RDB without replication buffer. */
435#define CLIENT_NO_EVICT (1ULL<<43) /* This client is protected against client
436 memory eviction. */
437#define CLIENT_ALLOW_OOM (1ULL<<44) /* Client used by RM_Call is allowed to fully execute
438 scripts even when in OOM */
439#define CLIENT_NO_TOUCH (1ULL<<45) /* This client will not touch LFU/LRU stats. */
440#define CLIENT_PUSHING (1ULL<<46) /* This client is pushing notifications. */
441#define CLIENT_MODULE_AUTH_HAS_RESULT (1ULL<<47) /* Indicates a client in the middle of module based
442 auth had been authenticated from the Module. */
443#define CLIENT_MODULE_PREVENT_AOF_PROP (1ULL<<48) /* Module client do not want to propagate to AOF */
444#define CLIENT_MODULE_PREVENT_REPL_PROP (1ULL<<49) /* Module client do not want to propagate to replica */
445#define CLIENT_REEXECUTING_COMMAND (1ULL<<50) /* The client is re-executing the command. */
446#define CLIENT_REPL_RDB_CHANNEL (1ULL<<51) /* Client which is used for rdb delivery as part of rdb channel replication */
447#define CLIENT_INTERNAL (1ULL<<52) /* Internal client connection */
448#define CLIENT_ASM_MIGRATING (1ULL<<53) /* Client is migrating RDB/stream data during atomic slot migration. */
449#define CLIENT_ASM_IMPORTING (1ULL<<54) /* Client is importing RDB/stream data during atomic slot migration. */
450
451/* Any flag that does not let optimize FLUSH SYNC to run it in bg as blocking client ASYNC */
452#define CLIENT_AVOID_BLOCKING_ASYNC_FLUSH (CLIENT_DENY_BLOCKING|CLIENT_MULTI|CLIENT_LUA_DEBUG|CLIENT_LUA_DEBUG_SYNC|CLIENT_MODULE)
453
454/* Max deferred objects to be freed by IO thread for each client. */
455#define CLIENT_MAX_DEFERRED_OBJECTS 32
456
457/* Client flags for client IO */
458#define CLIENT_IO_READ_ENABLED (1ULL<<0) /* Client can read from socket. */
459#define CLIENT_IO_WRITE_ENABLED (1ULL<<1) /* Client can write to socket. */
460#define CLIENT_IO_PENDING_COMMAND (1ULL<<2) /* Similar to CLIENT_PENDING_COMMAND. */
461#define CLIENT_IO_REUSABLE_QUERYBUFFER (1ULL<<3) /* The client is using the reusable query buffer. */
462#define CLIENT_IO_CLOSE_ASAP (1ULL<<4) /* Close this client ASAP in IO thread. */
463#define CLIENT_IO_PENDING_CRON (1ULL<<5) /* The client is pending cron job, to be processed in main thread. */
464
465/* Definitions for client read errors. These error codes are used to indicate
466 * various issues that can occur while reading or parsing data from a client. */
467#define CLIENT_READ_TOO_BIG_INLINE_REQUEST 1
468#define CLIENT_READ_UNBALANCED_QUOTES 2
469#define CLIENT_READ_MASTER_USING_INLINE_PROTOCAL 3
470#define CLIENT_READ_TOO_BIG_MBULK_COUNT_STRING 4
471#define CLIENT_READ_TOO_BIG_BUCK_COUNT_STRING 5
472#define CLIENT_READ_EXPECTED_DOLLAR 6
473#define CLIENT_READ_INVALID_BUCK_LENGTH 7
474#define CLIENT_READ_UNAUTH_BUCK_LENGTH 8
475#define CLIENT_READ_INVALID_MULTIBUCK_LENGTH 9
476#define CLIENT_READ_UNAUTH_MBUCK_COUNT 10
477#define CLIENT_READ_CONN_DISCONNECTED 11
478#define CLIENT_READ_CONN_CLOSED 12
479#define CLIENT_READ_REACHED_MAX_QUERYBUF 13
480#define CLIENT_READ_COMMAND_NOT_FOUND 14
481#define CLIENT_READ_BAD_ARITY 15
482#define CLIENT_READ_CROSS_SLOT 16
483
484/* Client block type (btype field in client structure)
485 * if CLIENT_BLOCKED flag is set. */
486typedef enum blocking_type {
487 BLOCKED_NONE, /* Not blocked, no CLIENT_BLOCKED flag set. */
488 BLOCKED_LIST, /* BLPOP & co. */
489 BLOCKED_WAIT, /* WAIT for synchronous replication. */
490 BLOCKED_WAITAOF, /* WAITAOF for AOF file fsync. */
491 BLOCKED_MODULE, /* Blocked by a loadable module. */
492 BLOCKED_STREAM, /* XREAD. */
493 BLOCKED_ZSET, /* BZPOP et al. */
494 BLOCKED_POSTPONE, /* Blocked by processCommand, re-try processing later. */
495 BLOCKED_POSTPONE_TRIM, /* Master client is blocked due to an active trim job. */
496 BLOCKED_SHUTDOWN, /* SHUTDOWN. */
497 BLOCKED_LAZYFREE, /* LAZYFREE */
498 BLOCKED_NUM, /* Number of blocked states. */
499 BLOCKED_END /* End of enumeration */
500} blocking_type;
501
502/* Client request types */
503#define PROTO_REQ_INLINE 1
504#define PROTO_REQ_MULTIBULK 2
505
506/* Client classes for client limits, currently used only for
507 * the max-client-output-buffer limit implementation. */
508#define CLIENT_TYPE_NORMAL 0 /* Normal req-reply clients + MONITORs */
509#define CLIENT_TYPE_SLAVE 1 /* Slaves. */
510#define CLIENT_TYPE_PUBSUB 2 /* Clients subscribed to PubSub channels. */
511#define CLIENT_TYPE_MASTER 3 /* Master. */
512#define CLIENT_TYPE_COUNT 4 /* Total number of client types. */
513#define CLIENT_TYPE_OBUF_COUNT 3 /* Number of clients to expose to output
514 buffer configuration. Just the first
515 three: normal, slave, pubsub. */
516
517/* Slave replication state. Used in server.repl_state for slaves to remember
518 * what to do next. */
519typedef enum {
520 REPL_STATE_NONE = 0, /* No active replication */
521 REPL_STATE_CONNECT, /* Must connect to master */
522 REPL_STATE_CONNECTING, /* Connecting to master */
523 /* --- Handshake states, must be ordered --- */
524 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_PING_REPLY, /* Wait for PING reply */
525 REPL_STATE_SEND_HANDSHAKE, /* Send handshake sequence to master */
526 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_AUTH_REPLY, /* Wait for AUTH reply */
527 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_PORT_REPLY, /* Wait for REPLCONF reply */
528 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_IP_REPLY, /* Wait for REPLCONF reply */
529 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_REQ_REPLY, /* Wait for REPLCONF reply */
530 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_CAPA_REPLY, /* Wait for REPLCONF reply */
531 REPL_STATE_SEND_PSYNC, /* Send PSYNC */
532 REPL_STATE_RECEIVE_PSYNC_REPLY, /* Wait for PSYNC reply */
533 /* --- End of handshake states --- */
534 REPL_STATE_TRANSFER, /* Receiving .rdb from master */
535 REPL_STATE_CONNECTED, /* Connected to master */
536} repl_state;
537
538/* Replica rdb channel replication state. Used in server.repl_rdb_ch_state for
539 * replicas to remember what to do next. */
540typedef enum {
541 REPL_RDB_CH_STATE_NONE = 0, /* No active rdb channel sync */
542 REPL_RDB_CH_SEND_HANDSHAKE, /* Send handshake sequence to master */
543 REPL_RDB_CH_RECEIVE_AUTH_REPLY, /* Wait for AUTH reply */
544 REPL_RDB_CH_RECEIVE_REPLCONF_REPLY, /* Wait for REPLCONF reply */
545 REPL_RDB_CH_RECEIVE_FULLRESYNC, /* Wait for +FULLRESYNC reply */
546 REPL_RDB_CH_RDB_LOADING, /* Loading rdb using rdb channel */
547} repl_rdb_channel_state;
548
549#define REPL_MAIN_CH_NONE (1 << 0)
550#define REPL_MAIN_CH_ACCUMULATE_BUF (1 << 1)
551#define REPL_MAIN_CH_STREAMING_BUF (1 << 2)
552#define REPL_MAIN_CH_CLOSE_ASAP (1 << 3)
553
554/* Replication debug flags for testing. */
555#define REPL_DEBUG_PAUSE_NONE (1 << 0)
556#define REPL_DEBUG_AFTER_FORK (1 << 1)
557#define REPL_DEBUG_BEFORE_RDB_CHANNEL (1 << 2)
558#define REPL_DEBUG_ON_STREAMING_REPL_BUF (1 << 3)
559
560/* The state of an in progress coordinated failover */
561typedef enum {
562 NO_FAILOVER = 0, /* No failover in progress */
563 FAILOVER_WAIT_FOR_SYNC, /* Waiting for target replica to catch up */
564 FAILOVER_IN_PROGRESS /* Waiting for target replica to accept
565 * PSYNC FAILOVER request. */
566} failover_state;
567
568/* State of slaves from the POV of the master. Used in client->replstate.
569 * In SEND_BULK and ONLINE state the slave receives new updates
570 * in its output queue. In the WAIT_BGSAVE states instead the server is waiting
571 * to start the next background saving in order to send updates to it. */
572#define SLAVE_STATE_WAIT_BGSAVE_START 6 /* We need to produce a new RDB file. */
573#define SLAVE_STATE_WAIT_BGSAVE_END 7 /* Waiting RDB file creation to finish. */
574#define SLAVE_STATE_SEND_BULK 8 /* Sending RDB file to slave. */
575#define SLAVE_STATE_ONLINE 9 /* RDB file transmitted, sending just updates. */
576#define SLAVE_STATE_RDB_TRANSMITTED 10 /* RDB file transmitted - This state is used only for
577 * a replica that only wants RDB without replication buffer */
578#define SLAVE_STATE_WAIT_RDB_CHANNEL 11 /* Main channel of replica is connected,
579 * we are waiting rdbchannel connection to start delivery.*/
580#define SLAVE_STATE_SEND_BULK_AND_STREAM 12 /* Main channel of a replica which uses rdb channel replication.
581 * Sending RDB file and replication stream in parallel. */
582
583/* Slave capabilities. */
584#define SLAVE_CAPA_NONE 0
585#define SLAVE_CAPA_EOF (1<<0) /* Can parse the RDB EOF streaming format. */
586#define SLAVE_CAPA_PSYNC2 (1<<1) /* Supports PSYNC2 protocol. */
587#define SLAVE_CAPA_RDB_CHANNEL_REPL (1<<2) /* Supports rdb channel replication during full sync */
588
589/* Slave requirements. NO_COMPRESS and NO_CHECKSUM are hints rather than strict
590 * requirements - the replica can handle compressed/checksummed RDB either way,
591 * but prefers to skip them for diskless loading since they become redundant.
592 * We reuse the REQ mechanism for simplicity, avoiding a separate HINT bitfield. */
593#define SLAVE_REQ_NONE 0
594#define SLAVE_REQ_RDB_EXCLUDE_DATA (1 << 0) /* Exclude data from RDB */
595#define SLAVE_REQ_RDB_EXCLUDE_FUNCTIONS (1 << 1) /* Exclude functions from RDB */
596#define SLAVE_REQ_SLOTS_SNAPSHOT (1 << 2) /* Only slots snapshot is required */
597#define SLAVE_REQ_RDB_CHANNEL (1 << 3) /* Use rdb channel replication, transfer RDB background */
598#define SLAVE_REQ_RDB_NO_COMPRESS (1 << 4) /* Don't enable RDB compression */
599#define SLAVE_REQ_RDB_NO_CHECKSUM (1 << 5) /* Don't enable RDB checksum */
600/* Mask of all bits in the slave requirements bitfield that represent non-standard (filtered) RDB requirements */
601#define SLAVE_REQ_RDB_MASK (SLAVE_REQ_RDB_EXCLUDE_DATA | SLAVE_REQ_RDB_EXCLUDE_FUNCTIONS | SLAVE_REQ_SLOTS_SNAPSHOT)
602
603/* Synchronous read timeout - slave side */
604#define CONFIG_REPL_SYNCIO_TIMEOUT 5
605
606/* The default number of replication backlog blocks to trim per call. */
607#define REPL_BACKLOG_TRIM_BLOCKS_PER_CALL 64
608
609/* In order to quickly find the requested offset for PSYNC requests,
610 * we index some nodes in the replication buffer linked list into a rax. */
611#define REPL_BACKLOG_INDEX_PER_BLOCKS 64
612
613/* List related stuff */
614#define LIST_HEAD 0
615#define LIST_TAIL 1
616#define ZSET_MIN 0
617#define ZSET_MAX 1
618
619/* Sort operations */
620#define SORT_OP_GET 0
621
622/* Log levels */
623#define LL_DEBUG 0
624#define LL_VERBOSE 1
625#define LL_NOTICE 2
626#define LL_WARNING 3
627#define LL_NOTHING 4
628#define LL_RAW (1<<10) /* Modifier to log without timestamp */
629
630/* Supervision options */
631#define SUPERVISED_NONE 0
632#define SUPERVISED_AUTODETECT 1
633#define SUPERVISED_SYSTEMD 2
634#define SUPERVISED_UPSTART 3
635
636/* Anti-warning macro... */
637#define UNUSED(V) ((void) V)
638
639#define ZSKIPLIST_MAXLEVEL 32 /* Should be enough for 2^64 elements */
640#define ZSKIPLIST_P 0.25 /* Skiplist P = 1/4 */
641#define ZSKIPLIST_MAX_SEARCH 10
642
643/* Append only defines */
644#define AOF_FSYNC_NO 0
645#define AOF_FSYNC_ALWAYS 1
646#define AOF_FSYNC_EVERYSEC 2
647
648/* Replication diskless load defines */
649#define REPL_DISKLESS_LOAD_DISABLED 0
650#define REPL_DISKLESS_LOAD_WHEN_DB_EMPTY 1
651#define REPL_DISKLESS_LOAD_SWAPDB 2
652#define REPL_DISKLESS_LOAD_ALWAYS 3
653
654/* TLS Client Authentication */
655#define TLS_CLIENT_AUTH_NO 0
656#define TLS_CLIENT_AUTH_YES 1
657#define TLS_CLIENT_AUTH_OPTIONAL 2
658
659/* TLS Client Certfiicate Authentication */
660#define TLS_CLIENT_FIELD_OFF 0
661#define TLS_CLIENT_FIELD_CN 1
662
663/* Sanitize dump payload */
664#define SANITIZE_DUMP_NO 0
665#define SANITIZE_DUMP_YES 1
666#define SANITIZE_DUMP_CLIENTS 2
667
668/* Enable protected config/command */
669#define PROTECTED_ACTION_ALLOWED_NO 0
670#define PROTECTED_ACTION_ALLOWED_YES 1
671#define PROTECTED_ACTION_ALLOWED_LOCAL 2
672
673/* Sets operations codes */
674#define SET_OP_UNION 0
675#define SET_OP_DIFF 1
676#define SET_OP_INTER 2
677
678/* oom-score-adj defines */
679#define OOM_SCORE_ADJ_NO 0
680#define OOM_SCORE_RELATIVE 1
681#define OOM_SCORE_ADJ_ABSOLUTE 2
682
683/* Redis maxmemory strategies. Instead of using just incremental number
684 * for this defines, we use a set of flags so that testing for certain
685 * properties common to multiple policies is faster. */
686#define MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRU (1<<0)
687#define MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LFU (1<<1)
688#define MAXMEMORY_FLAG_ALLKEYS (1<<2)
689#define MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRM (1<<3)
690#define MAXMEMORY_FLAG_NO_SHARED_INTEGERS \
691 (MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRU|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LFU|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRM)
692
693#define MAXMEMORY_VOLATILE_LRU ((0<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRU)
694#define MAXMEMORY_VOLATILE_LFU ((1<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LFU)
695#define MAXMEMORY_VOLATILE_TTL (2<<8)
696#define MAXMEMORY_VOLATILE_RANDOM (3<<8)
697#define MAXMEMORY_ALLKEYS_LRU ((4<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRU|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_ALLKEYS)
698#define MAXMEMORY_ALLKEYS_LFU ((5<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LFU|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_ALLKEYS)
699#define MAXMEMORY_ALLKEYS_RANDOM ((6<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_ALLKEYS)
700#define MAXMEMORY_NO_EVICTION (7<<8)
701#define MAXMEMORY_VOLATILE_LRM ((8<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRM)
702#define MAXMEMORY_ALLKEYS_LRM ((9<<8)|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_LRM|MAXMEMORY_FLAG_ALLKEYS)
703
704/* Units */
705#define UNIT_SECONDS 0
706#define UNIT_MILLISECONDS 1
707
708/* SHUTDOWN flags */
709#define SHUTDOWN_NOFLAGS 0 /* No flags. */
710#define SHUTDOWN_SAVE 1 /* Force SAVE on SHUTDOWN even if no save
711 points are configured. */
712#define SHUTDOWN_NOSAVE 2 /* Don't SAVE on SHUTDOWN. */
713#define SHUTDOWN_NOW 4 /* Don't wait for replicas to catch up. */
714#define SHUTDOWN_FORCE 8 /* Don't let errors prevent shutdown. */
715
716/* Cluster slot stats flags */
717#define CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_CPU 1 /* Track CPU usage per slot. */
718#define CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_NET 2 /* Track network bytes per slot. */
719#define CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_MEM 4 /* Track memory usage per slot. */
720#define CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_ALL (CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_CPU | CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_NET | CLUSTER_SLOT_STATS_MEM)
721
722/* IO thread pause status */
723#define IO_THREAD_UNPAUSED 0
724#define IO_THREAD_PAUSING 1
725#define IO_THREAD_PAUSED 2
726#define IO_THREAD_RESUMING 3
727
728/* Command call flags, see call() function */
729#define CMD_CALL_NONE 0
730#define CMD_CALL_PROPAGATE_AOF (1<<0)
731#define CMD_CALL_PROPAGATE_REPL (1<<1)
732#define CMD_CALL_FROM_MODULE (1<<2) /* From RM_Call */
733#define CMD_CALL_PROPAGATE (CMD_CALL_PROPAGATE_AOF|CMD_CALL_PROPAGATE_REPL)
734#define CMD_CALL_FULL (CMD_CALL_PROPAGATE)
735
736/* Command propagation flags, see propagateNow() function */
737#define PROPAGATE_NONE 0
738#define PROPAGATE_AOF 1
739#define PROPAGATE_REPL 2
740
741/* Actions pause types */
742#define PAUSE_ACTION_CLIENT_WRITE (1<<0)
743#define PAUSE_ACTION_CLIENT_ALL (1<<1) /* must be bigger than PAUSE_ACTION_CLIENT_WRITE */
744#define PAUSE_ACTION_EXPIRE (1<<2)
745#define PAUSE_ACTION_EVICT (1<<3)
746#define PAUSE_ACTION_REPLICA (1<<4) /* pause replica traffic */
747
748/* common sets of actions to pause/unpause */
749#define PAUSE_ACTIONS_CLIENT_WRITE_SET (PAUSE_ACTION_CLIENT_WRITE|\
750 PAUSE_ACTION_EXPIRE|\
751 PAUSE_ACTION_EVICT|\
752 PAUSE_ACTION_REPLICA)
753#define PAUSE_ACTIONS_CLIENT_ALL_SET (PAUSE_ACTION_CLIENT_ALL|\
754 PAUSE_ACTION_EXPIRE|\
755 PAUSE_ACTION_EVICT|\
756 PAUSE_ACTION_REPLICA)
757
758/* Client pause purposes. Each purpose has its own end time and pause type. */
759typedef enum {
760 PAUSE_BY_CLIENT_COMMAND = 0,
761 PAUSE_DURING_SHUTDOWN,
762 PAUSE_DURING_FAILOVER,
763 PAUSE_DURING_SLOT_HANDOFF,
764 NUM_PAUSE_PURPOSES /* This value is the number of purposes above. */
765} pause_purpose;
766
767typedef struct {
768 uint32_t paused_actions; /* Bitmask of actions */
769 mstime_t end;
770} pause_event;
771
772/* Ways that a clusters endpoint can be described */
773typedef enum {
774 CLUSTER_ENDPOINT_TYPE_IP = 0, /* Show IP address */
775 CLUSTER_ENDPOINT_TYPE_HOSTNAME, /* Show hostname */
776 CLUSTER_ENDPOINT_TYPE_UNKNOWN_ENDPOINT /* Show NULL or empty */
777} cluster_endpoint_type;
778
779/* RDB active child save type. */
780#define RDB_CHILD_TYPE_NONE 0
781#define RDB_CHILD_TYPE_DISK 1 /* RDB is written to disk. */
782#define RDB_CHILD_TYPE_SOCKET 2 /* RDB is written to slave socket. */
783
784/* Keyspace changes notification classes. Every class is associated with a
785 * character for configuration purposes. */
786#define NOTIFY_KEYSPACE (1<<0) /* K */
787#define NOTIFY_KEYEVENT (1<<1) /* E */
788#define NOTIFY_GENERIC (1<<2) /* g */
789#define NOTIFY_STRING (1<<3) /* $ */
790#define NOTIFY_LIST (1<<4) /* l */
791#define NOTIFY_SET (1<<5) /* s */
792#define NOTIFY_HASH (1<<6) /* h */
793#define NOTIFY_ZSET (1<<7) /* z */
794#define NOTIFY_EXPIRED (1<<8) /* x */
795#define NOTIFY_EVICTED (1<<9) /* e */
796#define NOTIFY_STREAM (1<<10) /* t */
797#define NOTIFY_KEY_MISS (1<<11) /* m (Note: This one is excluded from NOTIFY_ALL on purpose) */
798#define NOTIFY_LOADED (1<<12) /* module only key space notification, indicate a key loaded from rdb */
799#define NOTIFY_MODULE (1<<13) /* d, module key space notification */
800#define NOTIFY_NEW (1<<14) /* n, new key notification (Note: excluded from NOTIFY_ALL) */
801#define NOTIFY_OVERWRITTEN (1<<15) /* o, key overwrite notification (Note: excluded from NOTIFY_ALL) */
802#define NOTIFY_TYPE_CHANGED (1<<16) /* c, key type changed notification (Note: excluded from NOTIFY_ALL) */
803#define NOTIFY_KEY_TRIMMED (1<<17) /* module only key space notification, indicates a key trimmed during slot migration */
804#define NOTIFY_ALL (NOTIFY_GENERIC | NOTIFY_STRING | NOTIFY_LIST | NOTIFY_SET | NOTIFY_HASH | NOTIFY_ZSET | NOTIFY_EXPIRED | NOTIFY_EVICTED | NOTIFY_STREAM | NOTIFY_MODULE) /* A flag */
805
806/* Using the following macro you can run code inside serverCron() with the
807 * specified period, specified in milliseconds.
808 * The actual resolution depends on server.hz. */
809#define run_with_period(_ms_) if (((_ms_) <= 1000/server.hz) || !(server.cronloops%((_ms_)/(1000/server.hz))))
810
811/* We can print the stacktrace, so our assert is defined this way: */
812#define serverAssertWithInfo(_c,_o,_e) (likely(_e)?(void)0 : (_serverAssertWithInfo(_c,_o,#_e,__FILE__,__LINE__),redis_unreachable()))
813#define serverAssert(_e) (likely(_e)?(void)0 : (_serverAssert(#_e,__FILE__,__LINE__),redis_unreachable()))
814#define serverPanic(...) _serverPanic(__FILE__,__LINE__,__VA_ARGS__),redis_unreachable()
815
816/* The following macros provide assertions that are only executed during test builds and should be used to add
817 * assertions that are too computationally expensive or dangerous to run during normal operations. */
818#ifdef DEBUG_ASSERTIONS
819#define debugServerAssertWithInfo(...) serverAssertWithInfo(__VA_ARGS__)
820#define debugServerAssert(...) serverAssert(__VA_ARGS__)
821#else
822#define debugServerAssertWithInfo(...)
823#define debugServerAssert(...)
824#endif
825
826/* latency histogram per command init settings */
827#define LATENCY_HISTOGRAM_MIN_VALUE 1L /* >= 1 nanosec */
828#define LATENCY_HISTOGRAM_MAX_VALUE 1000000000L /* <= 1 secs */
829#define LATENCY_HISTOGRAM_PRECISION 2 /* Maintain a value precision of 2 significant digits across LATENCY_HISTOGRAM_MIN_VALUE and LATENCY_HISTOGRAM_MAX_VALUE range.
830 * Value quantization within the range will thus be no larger than 1/100th (or 1%) of any value.
831 * The total size per histogram should sit around 40 KiB Bytes. */
832
833/* Busy module flags, see busy_module_yield_flags */
834#define BUSY_MODULE_YIELD_NONE (0)
835#define BUSY_MODULE_YIELD_EVENTS (1<<0)
836#define BUSY_MODULE_YIELD_CLIENTS (1<<1)
837
838/* Key prefetch configs */
839#define PREFETCH_BATCH_MAX_SIZE 128
840
841/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
842 * Data types
843 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
844
845/* A redis object, that is a type able to hold a string / list / set */
846
847/* The actual Redis Object */
848#define OBJ_STRING 0 /* String object. */
849#define OBJ_LIST 1 /* List object. */
850#define OBJ_SET 2 /* Set object. */
851#define OBJ_ZSET 3 /* Sorted set object. */
852#define OBJ_HASH 4 /* Hash object. */
853#define OBJ_TYPE_BASIC_MAX 5 /* Max number of basic object types. */
854
855/* The "module" object type is a special one that signals that the object
856 * is one directly managed by a Redis module. In this case the value points
857 * to a moduleValue struct, which contains the object value (which is only
858 * handled by the module itself) and the RedisModuleType struct which lists
859 * function pointers in order to serialize, deserialize, AOF-rewrite and
860 * free the object.
861 *
862 * Inside the RDB file, module types are encoded as OBJ_MODULE followed
863 * by a 64 bit module type ID, which has a 54 bits module-specific signature
864 * in order to dispatch the loading to the right module, plus a 10 bits
865 * encoding version. */
866#define OBJ_MODULE 5 /* Module object. */
867#define OBJ_STREAM 6 /* Stream object. */
868#define OBJ_TYPE_MAX 7 /* Maximum number of object types */
869
870/* Extract encver / signature from a module type ID. */
871#define REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER_BITS 10
872#define REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER_MASK ((1<<REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER_BITS)-1)
873#define REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER(id) ((id) & REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER_MASK)
874#define REDISMODULE_TYPE_SIGN(id) (((id) & ~((uint64_t)REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER_MASK)) >>REDISMODULE_TYPE_ENCVER_BITS)
875
876/* Bit flags for moduleTypeAuxSaveFunc */
877#define REDISMODULE_AUX_BEFORE_RDB (1<<0)
878#define REDISMODULE_AUX_AFTER_RDB (1<<1)
879
880struct RedisModule;
881struct RedisModuleIO;
882struct RedisModuleDigest;
883struct RedisModuleCtx;
884struct moduleLoadQueueEntry;
885struct RedisModuleCommand;
886struct clusterState;
887struct slotRangeArray;
888
889/* Each module type implementation should export a set of methods in order
890 * to serialize and deserialize the value in the RDB file, rewrite the AOF
891 * log, create the digest for "DEBUG DIGEST", and free the value when a key
892 * is deleted. */
893typedef void *(*moduleTypeLoadFunc)(struct RedisModuleIO *io, int encver);
894typedef void (*moduleTypeSaveFunc)(struct RedisModuleIO *io, void *value);
895typedef int (*moduleTypeAuxLoadFunc)(struct RedisModuleIO *rdb, int encver, int when);
896typedef void (*moduleTypeAuxSaveFunc)(struct RedisModuleIO *rdb, int when);
897typedef void (*moduleTypeRewriteFunc)(struct RedisModuleIO *io, struct redisObject *key, void *value);
898typedef void (*moduleTypeDigestFunc)(struct RedisModuleDigest *digest, void *value);
899typedef size_t (*moduleTypeMemUsageFunc)(const void *value);
900typedef void (*moduleTypeFreeFunc)(void *value);
901typedef size_t (*moduleTypeFreeEffortFunc)(struct redisObject *key, const void *value);
902typedef void (*moduleTypeUnlinkFunc)(struct redisObject *key, void *value);
903typedef void *(*moduleTypeCopyFunc)(struct redisObject *fromkey, struct redisObject *tokey, const void *value);
904typedef int (*moduleTypeDefragFunc)(struct RedisModuleDefragCtx *ctx, struct redisObject *key, void **value);
905typedef size_t (*moduleTypeMemUsageFunc2)(struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, const void *value, size_t sample_size);
906typedef void (*moduleTypeFreeFunc2)(struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, void *value);
907typedef size_t (*moduleTypeFreeEffortFunc2)(struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, const void *value);
908typedef void (*moduleTypeUnlinkFunc2)(struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, void *value);
909typedef void *(*moduleTypeCopyFunc2)(struct RedisModuleKeyOptCtx *ctx, const void *value);
910typedef int (*moduleTypeAuthCallback)(struct RedisModuleCtx *ctx, void *username, void *password, const char **err);
911
912/* Module Entity ID: module type or keymeta. */
913typedef struct ModuleEntityId {
914 struct RedisModule *module;
915 char name[10]; /* 9 bytes name + null term. Charset: A-Z a-z 0-9 _- */
916 uint64_t id; /* Higher 54 bits of type ID + 10 lower bits of encoding ver. */
917} ModuleEntityId;
918
919/* The module type, which is referenced in each value of a given type, defines
920 * the methods and links to the module exporting the type. */
921typedef struct RedisModuleType {
922 ModuleEntityId entity; /* module data type name and ID. */
923 moduleTypeLoadFunc rdb_load;
924 moduleTypeSaveFunc rdb_save;
925 moduleTypeRewriteFunc aof_rewrite;
926 moduleTypeMemUsageFunc mem_usage;
927 moduleTypeDigestFunc digest;
928 moduleTypeFreeFunc free;
929 moduleTypeFreeEffortFunc free_effort;
930 moduleTypeUnlinkFunc unlink;
931 moduleTypeCopyFunc copy;
932 moduleTypeDefragFunc defrag;
933 moduleTypeAuxLoadFunc aux_load;
934 moduleTypeAuxSaveFunc aux_save;
935 moduleTypeMemUsageFunc2 mem_usage2;
936 moduleTypeFreeEffortFunc2 free_effort2;
937 moduleTypeUnlinkFunc2 unlink2;
938 moduleTypeCopyFunc2 copy2;
939 moduleTypeAuxSaveFunc aux_save2;
940 int aux_save_triggers;
941} moduleType;
942
943/* In Redis objects 'robj' structures of type OBJ_MODULE, the value pointer
944 * is set to the following structure, referencing the moduleType structure
945 * in order to work with the value, and at the same time providing a raw
946 * pointer to the value, as created by the module commands operating with
947 * the module type.
948 *
949 * So for example in order to free such a value, it is possible to use
950 * the following code:
951 *
952 * if (robj->type == OBJ_MODULE) {
953 * moduleValue *mt = robj->ptr;
954 * mt->type->free(mt->value);
955 * zfree(mt); // We need to release this in-the-middle struct as well.
956 * }
957 */
958typedef struct moduleValue {
959 moduleType *type;
960 void *value;
961} moduleValue;
962
963/* Describe the state of the module during loading, and the indication which configs were loaded / applied already. */
964typedef enum {
965 MODULE_CONFIGS_DEFAULTS = 0x1, /* The registered defaults were applied. */
966 MODULE_CONFIGS_USER_VALS = 0x2, /* The user provided values were applied. */
967 MODULE_CONFIGS_ALL_APPLIED = 0x3 /* Both of the above applied. */
968} ModuleConfigsApplied;
969
970/* This structure represents a module inside the system. */
971struct RedisModule {
972 void *handle; /* Module dlopen() handle. */
973 char *name; /* Module name. */
974 int ver; /* Module version. We use just progressive integers. */
975 int apiver; /* Module API version as requested during initialization.*/
976 list *types; /* Module data types. */
977 list *usedby; /* List of modules using APIs from this one. */
978 list *using; /* List of modules we use some APIs of. */
979 list *filters; /* List of filters the module has registered. */
980 list *module_configs; /* List of configurations the module has registered */
981 ModuleConfigsApplied configs_initialized; /* Have the module configurations been initialized? */
982 int in_call; /* RM_Call() nesting level */
983 int in_hook; /* Hooks callback nesting level for this module (0 or 1). */
984 int options; /* Module options and capabilities. */
985 int blocked_clients; /* Count of RedisModuleBlockedClient in this module. */
986 RedisModuleInfoFunc info_cb; /* Callback for module to add INFO fields. */
987 RedisModuleDefragFunc defrag_cb; /* Callback for global data defrag. */
988 RedisModuleDefragFunc2 defrag_cb_2; /* Version 2 callback for global data defrag. */
989 RedisModuleDefragFunc defrag_start_cb; /* Callback indicating defrag started. */
990 RedisModuleDefragFunc defrag_end_cb; /* Callback indicating defrag ended. */
991 struct moduleLoadQueueEntry *loadmod; /* Module load arguments for config rewrite. */
992 int num_commands_with_acl_categories; /* Number of commands in this module included in acl categories */
993 int onload; /* Flag to identify if the call is being made from Onload (0 or 1) */
994 size_t num_acl_categories_added; /* Number of acl categories added by this module. */
995};
996typedef struct RedisModule RedisModule;
997
998/* The defrag context, used to manage state during calls to the data type
999 * defrag callback.
1000 */
1001struct RedisModuleDefragCtx {
1002 monotime endtime;
1003 unsigned long *cursor;
1004 struct redisObject *key; /* Optional name of key processed, NULL when unknown. */
1005 int dbid; /* The dbid of the key being processed, -1 when unknown. */
1006 long long last_stop_check_hits; /* Number of defrag hits at last check. */
1007 long long last_stop_check_misses; /* Number of defrag misses at last check. */
1008 int stopping; /* Flag indicating if defrag should stop. */
1009};
1010#define INIT_MODULE_DEFRAG_CTX(endtime, cursor, key, dbid) \
1011 ((RedisModuleDefragCtx) { \
1012 (endtime), (cursor), (key), (dbid), \
1013 server.stat_active_defrag_hits, \
1014 server.stat_active_defrag_misses \
1015 })
1016
1017/* This is a wrapper for the 'rio' streams used inside rdb.c in Redis, so that
1018 * the user does not have to take the total count of the written bytes nor
1019 * to care about error conditions. */
1020struct RedisModuleIO {
1021 size_t bytes; /* Bytes read / written so far. */
1022 rio *rio; /* Rio stream. */
1023 ModuleEntityId *entity; /* Module type or keymeta doing the operation. */
1024 int error; /* True if error condition happened. */
1025 struct RedisModuleCtx *ctx; /* Optional context, see RM_GetContextFromIO()*/
1026 struct redisObject *key; /* Optional name of key processed */
1027 int dbid; /* The dbid of the key being processed, -1 when unknown. */
1028 sds pre_flush_buffer; /* A buffer that should be flushed before next write operation
1029 * See rdbSaveSingleModuleAux for more details */
1030};
1031
1032/* Initialize an IO context. Note that the 'ver' field is populated
1033 * inside rdb.c according to the version of the value to load. */
1034static inline void moduleInitIOContext(RedisModuleIO *io, ModuleEntityId *entity,
1035 rio *rioptr, struct redisObject *keyptr, int db)
1036{
1037 io->rio = rioptr;
1038 io->entity = entity;
1039 io->bytes = 0;
1040 io->error = 0;
1041 io->key = keyptr;
1042 io->dbid = db;
1043 io->ctx = NULL;
1044 io->pre_flush_buffer = NULL;
1045}
1046
1047/* This is a structure used to export DEBUG DIGEST capabilities to Redis
1048 * modules. We want to capture both the ordered and unordered elements of
1049 * a data structure, so that a digest can be created in a way that correctly
1050 * reflects the values. See the DEBUG DIGEST command implementation for more
1051 * background. */
1052struct RedisModuleDigest {
1053 unsigned char o[20]; /* Ordered elements. */
1054 unsigned char x[20]; /* Xored elements. */
1055 struct redisObject *key; /* Optional name of key processed */
1056 int dbid; /* The dbid of the key being processed */
1057};
1058
1059/* Just start with a digest composed of all zero bytes. */
1060#define moduleInitDigestContext(mdvar) do { \
1061 memset(mdvar.o,0,sizeof(mdvar.o)); \
1062 memset(mdvar.x,0,sizeof(mdvar.x)); \
1063} while(0)
1064
1065/* Macro to check if the client is in the middle of module based authentication. */
1066#define clientHasModuleAuthInProgress(c) ((c)->module_auth_ctx != NULL)
1067
1068/* The string name for an object's type as listed above
1069 * Native types are checked against the OBJ_STRING, OBJ_LIST, OBJ_* defines,
1070 * and Module types have their registered name returned. */
1071char *getObjectTypeName(robj*);
1072
1073/* Macro used to initialize a Redis object allocated on the stack.
1074 * Note that this macro is taken near the structure definition to make sure
1075 * we'll update it when the structure is changed, to avoid bugs like
1076 * bug #85 introduced exactly in this way. */
1077#define initStaticStringObject(_var,_ptr) do { \
1078 _var.refcount = OBJ_STATIC_REFCOUNT; \
1079 _var.type = OBJ_STRING; \
1080 _var.encoding = OBJ_ENCODING_RAW; \
1081 _var.metabits = 0; \
1082 _var.iskvobj = 0; \
1083 _var.ptr = _ptr; \
1084} while(0)
1085
1086struct evictionPoolEntry; /* Defined in evict.c */
1087
1088/* Encoded buffers contain headers followed by either plain replies or
1089 * by bulk string references */
1090typedef enum {
1091 PLAIN_REPLY = 0, /* plain reply */
1092 BULK_STR_REF /* bulk string references */
1093} payloadType;
1094
1095/* Encoded reply buffers consist of chunks
1096 * Each chunk contains header followed by payload
1097 * The packed attribute is specified because buffer is accessed at arbitrary offsets,
1098 * so no benefit in data structure padding and applying packed saves the space in the buffer */
1099typedef struct __attribute__((__packed__)) payloadHeader {
1100 size_t payload_len; /* payload length in a reply buffer */
1101 uint8_t payload_type; /* one of payloadType */
1102} payloadHeader;
1103static_assert(offsetof(payloadHeader, payload_len) == 0, "payload_len must be at offset 0 to avoid unaligned access");
1104
1105/* To avoid copy of whole string in reply buffer
1106 * we store pointers to object and string itself */
1107typedef struct __attribute__((__packed__)) bulkStrRef {
1108 robj *obj; /* pointer to object used for reference count management */
1109 unsigned int prefix_cnt;
1110 char prefix[LONG_STR_SIZE + 3]; /* $<len>\r\n */
1111 char crlf[2]; /* \r\n */
1112} bulkStrRef;
1113
1114/* This structure is used in order to represent the output buffer of a client,
1115 * which is actually a linked list of blocks like that, that is: client->reply. */
1116typedef struct clientReplyBlock {
1117 size_t size, used;
1118 char buf_encoded;
1119 char buf[];
1120} clientReplyBlock;
1121
1122/* Replication buffer blocks is the list of replBufBlock.
1123 *
1124 * +--------------+ +--------------+ +--------------+
1125 * | refcount = 1 | ... | refcount = 0 | ... | refcount = 2 |
1126 * +--------------+ +--------------+ +--------------+
1127 * | / \
1128 * | / \
1129 * | / \
1130 * Repl Backlog Replica_A Replica_B
1131 *
1132 * Each replica or replication backlog increments only the refcount of the
1133 * 'ref_repl_buf_node' which it points to. So when replica walks to the next
1134 * node, it should first increase the next node's refcount, and when we trim
1135 * the replication buffer nodes, we remove node always from the head node which
1136 * refcount is 0. If the refcount of the head node is not 0, we must stop
1137 * trimming and never iterate the next node.
1138 *
1139 * For replicas in IO threads we don't update the refcount while sending the
1140 * repl data, but only when the client is sent back to main. This avoids data
1141 * races. In order to achieve this, the replicas keep track of following:
1142 * - io_curr_repl_node - the current node we've reached.
1143 * - io_bound_repl_node - the last node in the replication buffer as seen by
1144 * the replica client before it was sent to IO thread
1145 *
1146 * When the client is sent to IO thread for the first time io_curr_repl_node is
1147 * initialized with ref_repl_buf_node.
1148 * When the client is sent back to main it can decrement ref_repl_buf_node's
1149 * refcount and increment it for io_curr_repl_node, since all the nodes
1150 * in-between are already sent and the client doesn't hold reference to them.
1151 *
1152 * `io_bound_repl_node` is needed because IO thread needs to know when to stop
1153 * sending data. If it was reading directly from the replication buffer,
1154 * there will be a data race, because main thread may be writing to it during
1155 * `feedReplicationBuffer`. `io_bound_repl_node` is cached in the client
1156 * together with its used size just before sending the client to IO thread
1157 * in `enqueuePendingClienstToIOThreads`. */
1158
1159/* Similar with 'clientReplyBlock', it is used for shared buffers between
1160 * all replica clients and replication backlog. */
1161typedef struct replBufBlock {
1162 int refcount; /* Number of replicas or repl backlog using. */
1163 long long id; /* The unique incremental number. */
1164 long long repl_offset; /* Start replication offset of the block. */
1165 size_t size; /* Capacity of the buf in bytes */
1166 size_t used; /* Count of written bytes */
1167 char buf[];
1168} replBufBlock;
1169
1170/* Redis database representation. There are multiple databases identified
1171 * by integers from 0 (the default database) up to the max configured
1172 * database. The database number is the 'id' field in the structure. */
1173typedef struct redisDb {
1174 kvstore *keys; /* The keyspace for this DB. As metadata, holds keysizes histogram */
1175 kvstore *expires; /* Timeout of keys with a timeout set */
1176 estore *subexpires; /* Timeout of sub-keys with a timeout set. (Currently only used for hashes) */
1177 dict *blocking_keys; /* Keys with clients waiting for data (BLPOP)*/
1178 dict *blocking_keys_unblock_on_nokey; /* Keys with clients waiting for
1179 * data, and should be unblocked if key is deleted (XREADEDGROUP).
1180 * This is a subset of blocking_keys*/
1181 dict *stream_claim_pending_keys; /* Keys with clients waiting to claim pending entries */
1182 dict *stream_idmp_keys; /* Stream keys with IDMP tracking */
1183 dict *ready_keys; /* Blocked keys that received a PUSH */
1184 dict *watched_keys; /* WATCHED keys for MULTI/EXEC CAS */
1185 int id; /* Database ID */
1186 long long avg_ttl; /* Average TTL, just for stats */
1187 unsigned long expires_cursor; /* Cursor of the active expire cycle. */
1188} redisDb;
1189
1190/* maximum number of bins of keysizes histogram */
1191#define MAX_KEYSIZES_BINS 60
1192#define MAX_KEYSIZES_TYPES 5 /* static_assert at db.c verifies == OBJ_TYPE_BASIC_MAX */
1193typedef int64_t keysizesHist[MAX_KEYSIZES_TYPES][MAX_KEYSIZES_BINS];
1194
1195/* Metadata structure used for kvstores with type `kvstoreExType`, managed outside kvstore */
1196typedef struct {
1197 keysizesHist keysizes_hist;
1198 keysizesHist allocsizes_hist;
1199} kvstoreMetadata;
1200
1201/* Like kvstoreMetadata, this one per dict */
1202typedef struct {
1203 kvstoreDictMetaBase base; /* must be first in struct ! */
1204 size_t alloc_size; /* Total memory used (in bytes) by this slot */
1205 uint64_t cpu_usec; /* CPU time (in microseconds) spent on given slot */
1206 uint64_t network_bytes_in; /* Network ingress (in bytes) received for given slot */
1207 uint64_t network_bytes_out; /* Network egress (in bytes) sent for given slot */
1208} kvstoreDictMetadata;
1209
1210/* Context for ASM background trim with delta histogram tracking */
1211typedef struct asmTrimCtx {
1212 int refcount; /* For shared bg/main thread ownership */
1213 struct slotRangeArray *slots; /* Slot ranges being trimmed */
1214 kvstore *target_kvstore; /* Target kvstore to update (for validation) */
1215 keysizesHist delta_keysizes_hist; /* Delta populated by BIO thread */
1216 keysizesHist delta_allocsizes_hist;/* Delta populated by BIO thread */
1217} asmTrimCtx;
1218
1219/* forward declaration for functions ctx */
1220typedef struct functionsLibCtx functionsLibCtx;
1221
1222/* Holding object that need to be populated during
1223 * rdb loading. On loading end it is possible to decide
1224 * whether not to set those objects on their rightful place.
1225 * For example: dbarray need to be set as main database on
1226 * successful loading and dropped on failure. */
1227typedef struct rdbLoadingCtx {
1228 redisDb* dbarray;
1229 functionsLibCtx* functions_lib_ctx;
1230}rdbLoadingCtx;
1231
1232typedef struct pendingCommand pendingCommand;
1233typedef struct multiState {
1234 pendingCommand **commands; /* Array of pointers to MULTI commands */
1235 int executing_cmd; /* The index of the currently executed transaction
1236 command (index in commands field) */
1237 int count; /* Total number of MULTI commands */
1238 int cmd_flags; /* The accumulated command flags OR-ed together.
1239 So if at least a command has a given flag, it
1240 will be set in this field. */
1241 int cmd_inv_flags; /* Same as cmd_flags, OR-ing the ~flags. so that it
1242 is possible to know if all the commands have a
1243 certain flag. */
1244 size_t argv_len_sums; /* mem used by all commands arguments */
1245 int alloc_count; /* total number of pendingCommand struct memory reserved. */
1246} multiState;
1247
1248/* This structure holds the blocking operation state for a client.
1249 * The fields used depend on client->btype. */
1250typedef struct blockingState {
1251 /* Generic fields. */
1252 blocking_type btype; /* Type of blocking op if CLIENT_BLOCKED. */
1253 mstime_t timeout; /* Blocking operation timeout. If UNIX current time
1254 * is > timeout then the operation timed out. */
1255 int unblock_on_nokey; /* Whether to unblock the client when at least one of the keys
1256 is deleted or does not exist anymore */
1257 /* BLOCKED_LIST, BLOCKED_ZSET and BLOCKED_STREAM or any other Keys related blocking */
1258 dict *keys; /* The keys we are blocked on */
1259
1260 /* BLOCKED_WAIT and BLOCKED_WAITAOF */
1261 int numreplicas; /* Number of replicas we are waiting for ACK. */
1262 int numlocal; /* Indication if WAITAOF is waiting for local fsync. */
1263 long long reploffset; /* Replication offset to reach. */
1264
1265 /* BLOCKED_MODULE */
1266 void *module_blocked_handle; /* RedisModuleBlockedClient structure.
1267 which is opaque for the Redis core, only
1268 handled in module.c. */
1269
1270 void *async_rm_call_handle; /* RedisModuleAsyncRMCallPromise structure.
1271 which is opaque for the Redis core, only
1272 handled in module.c. */
1273
1274 /* BLOCKED_LAZYFREE */
1275 monotime lazyfreeStartTime;
1276} blockingState;
1277
1278/* The following structure represents a node in the server.ready_keys list,
1279 * where we accumulate all the keys that had clients blocked with a blocking
1280 * operation such as B[LR]POP, but received new data in the context of the
1281 * last executed command.
1282 *
1283 * After the execution of every command or script, we iterate over this list to check
1284 * if as a result we should serve data to clients blocked, unblocking them.
1285 * Note that server.ready_keys will not have duplicates as there dictionary
1286 * also called ready_keys in every structure representing a Redis database,
1287 * where we make sure to remember if a given key was already added in the
1288 * server.ready_keys list. */
1289typedef struct readyList {
1290 redisDb *db;
1291 robj *key;
1292} readyList;
1293
1294/* List of pending commands. */
1295typedef struct pendingCommandList {
1296 pendingCommand *head;
1297 pendingCommand *tail;
1298 int len; /* Number of commands in the list */
1299 int ready_len; /* Number of commands that are ready to be processed */
1300} pendingCommandList;
1301
1302/* Pending command pool management structure */
1303#define PENDING_COMMAND_POOL_SIZE 16
1304#define PENDING_COMMAND_POOL_MAX_SIZE 1024
1305typedef struct pendingCommandPool {
1306 pendingCommand **pool; /* Pool array for reusing pendingCommand objects */
1307 int size; /* Current number of objects in pool */
1308 int capacity; /* Current capacity of the pool array */
1309 int min_size; /* Minimum size since last check (indicates peak usage) */
1310} pendingCommandPool;
1311
1312/* This structure represents a Redis user. This is useful for ACLs, the
1313 * user is associated to the connection after the connection is authenticated.
1314 * If there is no associated user, the connection uses the default user. */
1315#define USER_COMMAND_BITS_COUNT 1024 /* The total number of command bits
1316 in the user structure. The last valid
1317 command ID we can set in the user
1318 is USER_COMMAND_BITS_COUNT-1. */
1319#define USER_FLAG_ENABLED (1<<0) /* The user is active. */
1320#define USER_FLAG_DISABLED (1<<1) /* The user is disabled. */
1321#define USER_FLAG_NOPASS (1<<2) /* The user requires no password, any
1322 provided password will work. For the
1323 default user, this also means that
1324 no AUTH is needed, and every
1325 connection is immediately
1326 authenticated. */
1327#define USER_FLAG_SANITIZE_PAYLOAD (1<<3) /* The user require a deep RESTORE
1328 * payload sanitization. */
1329#define USER_FLAG_SANITIZE_PAYLOAD_SKIP (1<<4) /* The user should skip the
1330 * deep sanitization of RESTORE
1331 * payload. */
1332
1333#define SELECTOR_FLAG_ROOT (1<<0) /* This is the root user permission
1334 * selector. */
1335#define SELECTOR_FLAG_ALLKEYS (1<<1) /* The user can mention any key. */
1336#define SELECTOR_FLAG_ALLCOMMANDS (1<<2) /* The user can run all commands. */
1337#define SELECTOR_FLAG_ALLCHANNELS (1<<3) /* The user can mention any Pub/Sub
1338 channel. */
1339
1340typedef struct {
1341 sds name; /* The username as an SDS string. */
1342 redisAtomic uint32_t flags; /* See USER_FLAG_* */
1343 list *passwords; /* A list of SDS valid passwords for this user. */
1344 list *selectors; /* A list of selectors this user validates commands
1345 against. This list will always contain at least
1346 one selector for backwards compatibility. */
1347 robj *acl_string; /* cached string represent of ACLs */
1348} user;
1349
1350/* With multiplexing we need to take per-client state.
1351 * Clients are taken in a linked list. */
1352
1353#define CLIENT_ID_AOF (UINT64_MAX) /* Reserved ID for the AOF client. If you
1354 need more reserved IDs use UINT64_MAX-1,
1355 -2, ... and so forth. */
1356#define CLIENT_ID_NONE (0) /* Non-existent client ID, used when no client
1357 is associated with an operation. */
1358
1359/* Replication backlog is not a separate memory, it just is one consumer of
1360 * the global replication buffer. This structure records the reference of
1361 * replication buffers. Since the replication buffer block list may be very long,
1362 * it would cost much time to search replication offset on partial resync, so
1363 * we use one rax tree to index some blocks every REPL_BACKLOG_INDEX_PER_BLOCKS
1364 * to make searching offset from replication buffer blocks list faster. */
1365typedef struct replBacklog {
1366 listNode *ref_repl_buf_node; /* Referenced node of replication buffer blocks,
1367 * see the definition of replBufBlock. */
1368 size_t unindexed_count; /* The count from last creating index block. */
1369 rax *blocks_index; /* The index of recorded blocks of replication
1370 * buffer for quickly searching replication
1371 * offset on partial resynchronization. */
1372 long long histlen; /* Backlog actual data length */
1373 long long offset; /* Replication "master offset" of first
1374 * byte in the replication backlog buffer.*/
1375} replBacklog;
1376
1377/* Used by replDataBuf during rdb channel replication to accumulate replication
1378 * stream on replica side. */
1379typedef struct replDataBufBlock {
1380 size_t used; /* Used bytes in the buf */
1381 size_t size; /* Size of the buf */
1382 char buf[]; /* Replication data */
1383} replDataBufBlock;
1384
1385/* Linked list of replDataBufBlock structs, holds replication stream during
1386 * rdb channel replication on replica side. */
1387typedef struct replDataBuf {
1388 list *blocks; /* List of replDataBufBlock */
1389 size_t mem_used; /* Total allocated memory */
1390 size_t size; /* Total number of bytes available in all blocks. */
1391 size_t used; /* Total number of bytes actually used in all blocks. */
1392 size_t peak; /* Peak number of bytes stored in all blocks. */
1393 size_t last_num_blocks; /* Used to verify we consume more than we read from
1394 * the master connection while streaming buffer to
1395 * the db. */
1396} replDataBuf;
1397
1398typedef struct {
1399 list *clients;
1400 size_t mem_usage_sum;
1401} clientMemUsageBucket;
1402
1403#define DEFERRED_OBJECT_TYPE_PENDING_COMMAND 1
1404#define DEFERRED_OBJECT_TYPE_ROBJ 2
1405/* Structure to hold objects that need to be freed later by IO threads.
1406 * This allows the main thread to defer memory cleanup operations to
1407 * IO threads to avoid blocking the main event loop. */
1408typedef struct deferredObject {
1409 int type; /* Pointer to the object to be freed */
1410 void *ptr; /* Type of object: DEFERRED_OBJECT_TYPE_* */
1411} deferredObject;
1412
1413#define SHOULD_CLUSTER_COMPATIBILITY_SAMPLE() \
1414 (server.cluster_compatibility_sample_ratio == 100 || \
1415 (double)rand()/RAND_MAX * 100 < server.cluster_compatibility_sample_ratio)
1416
1417#ifdef LOG_REQ_RES
1418/* Structure used to log client's requests and their
1419 * responses (see logreqres.c) */
1420typedef struct {
1421 /* General */
1422 int argv_logged; /* 1 if the command was logged */
1423 /* Vars for log buffer */
1424 unsigned char *buf; /* Buffer holding the data (request and response) */
1425 size_t used;
1426 size_t capacity;
1427 /* Vars for offsets within the client's reply */
1428 struct {
1429 /* General */
1430 int saved; /* 1 if we already saved the offset (first time we call addReply*) */
1431 /* Offset within the static reply buffer */
1432 size_t bufpos;
1433 /* Offset within the reply block list */
1434 struct {
1435 int index;
1436 size_t used;
1437 } last_node;
1438 } offset;
1439} clientReqResInfo;
1440#endif
1441
1442typedef struct client {
1443 uint64_t id; /* Client incremental unique ID. */
1444 uint64_t flags; /* Client flags: CLIENT_* macros. */
1445 connection *conn;
1446 uint8_t tid; /* Thread assigned ID this client is bound to. */
1447 uint8_t running_tid; /* Thread assigned ID this client is running on. */
1448 uint8_t io_flags; /* Accessed by both main and IO threads, but not modified concurrently */
1449 uint8_t read_error; /* Client read error: CLIENT_READ_* macros. */
1450 int resp; /* RESP protocol version. Can be 2 or 3. */
1451 redisDb *db; /* Pointer to currently SELECTed DB. */
1452 robj *name; /* As set by CLIENT SETNAME. */
1453 robj *lib_name; /* The client library name as set by CLIENT SETINFO. */
1454 robj *lib_ver; /* The client library version as set by CLIENT SETINFO. */
1455 sds querybuf; /* Buffer we use to accumulate client queries. */
1456 size_t qb_pos; /* The position we have read in querybuf. */
1457 size_t querybuf_peak; /* Recent (100ms or more) peak of querybuf size. */
1458 int argc; /* Num of arguments of current command. */
1459 robj **argv; /* Arguments of current command. */
1460 int mls_argv; /* MLS handle for arguments. */
1461 int argv_len; /* Size of argv array (may be more than argc) */
1462 int original_argc; /* Num of arguments of original command if arguments were rewritten. */
1463 robj **original_argv; /* Arguments of original command if arguments were rewritten. */
1464 size_t all_argv_len_sum; /* Sum of lengths of objects in all pendingCommand argv lists */
1465 pendingCommandList pending_cmds; /* List of parsed pending commands */
1466 pendingCommand *current_pending_cmd;
1467 deferredObject *deferred_objects; /* Array of deferred objects to free. */
1468 int deferred_objects_num; /* Number of deferred objects to free. */
1469 robj **io_deferred_objects; /* Objects to be freed by main thread, queued by IO thread */
1470 int io_deferred_objects_num; /* Number of objects in io_deferred_objects */
1471 int io_deferred_objects_size; /* Allocated size of io_deferred_objects */
1472 struct redisCommand *cmd, *lastcmd; /* Last command executed. */
1473 struct redisCommand *lookedcmd; /* Command looked up in lookahead. */
1474 struct redisCommand *realcmd; /* The original command that was executed by the client,
1475 Used to update error stats in case the c->cmd was modified
1476 during the command invocation (like on GEOADD for example). */
1477 user *user; /* User associated with this connection. If the
1478 user is set to NULL the connection can do
1479 anything (admin). */
1480 int reqtype; /* Request protocol type: PROTO_REQ_* */
1481 int multibulklen; /* Number of multi bulk arguments left to read. */
1482 long bulklen; /* Length of bulk argument in multi bulk request. */
1483 list *reply; /* List of reply objects to send to the client. */
1484 unsigned long long reply_bytes; /* Tot bytes of objects in reply list. */
1485 list *deferred_reply_errors; /* Used for module thread safe contexts. */
1486 size_t sentlen; /* Amount of bytes already sent in the current
1487 buffer or object being sent. */
1488 time_t ctime; /* Client creation time. */
1489 long duration; /* Current command duration. Used for measuring latency of blocking/non-blocking cmds */
1490 int slot; /* The slot the client is executing against. Set to -1 if no slot is being used */
1491 int cluster_compatibility_check_slot; /* The slot the client is executing against for cluster compatibility check.
1492 * -2 means we don't need to check slot violation, or we already found
1493 * a violation, reported it and don't need to continue checking.
1494 * -1 means we're looking for the slot number and didn't find it yet.
1495 * any positive number means we found a slot and no violation yet. */
1496 dictEntry *cur_script; /* Cached pointer to the dictEntry of the script being executed. */
1497 time_t lastinteraction; /* Time of the last interaction, used for timeout */
1498 time_t io_lastinteraction; /* Time of the last interaction as seen from
1499 * IO thread. When the client is moved to main
1500 * it updates its `lastinteraction` value from
1501 * this. */
1502 time_t obuf_soft_limit_reached_time;
1503 mstime_t io_last_client_cron; /* Timestamp of last invocation of client
1504 * cron if client is running in IO thread */
1505 mstime_t io_last_repl_cron; /* Timestamp of last invocation of replication
1506 * cron if client is running in IO thread. */
1507 int authenticated; /* Needed when the default user requires auth. */
1508 int replstate; /* Replication state if this is a slave. */
1509 int repl_start_cmd_stream_on_ack; /* Install slave write handler on first ACK. */
1510 int repldbfd; /* Replication DB file descriptor. */
1511 off_t repldboff; /* Replication DB file offset. */
1512 off_t repldbsize; /* Replication DB file size. */
1513 sds replpreamble; /* Replication DB preamble. */
1514 long long read_reploff; /* Read replication offset if this is a master. */
1515 long long io_read_reploff; /* Copy of read_reploff but only used when
1516 * master client is in IO thread so we don't
1517 * have contention with IO thread. */
1518 long long reploff; /* Applied replication offset if this is a master. */
1519 long long reploff_next; /* Next value to set for reploff when a command finishes executing */
1520 long long repl_applied; /* Applied replication data count in querybuf, if this is a replica. */
1521 long long repl_ack_off; /* Replication ack offset, if this is a slave. */
1522 long long repl_aof_off; /* Replication AOF fsync ack offset, if this is a slave. */
1523 long long repl_ack_time;/* Replication ack time, if this is a slave. */
1524 long long io_repl_ack_time; /* Replication ack time, if this is a replica in
1525 * IO thread. Keeps track of repl_ack_time while
1526 * replica is in IO thread to avoid data races
1527 * with main. repl_ack_time is updated with this
1528 * value when replica returns to main thread. */
1529 long long repl_last_partial_write; /* The last time the server did a partial write from the RDB child pipe to this replica */
1530 long long psync_initial_offset; /* FULLRESYNC reply offset other slaves
1531 copying this slave output buffer
1532 should use. */
1533 char replid[CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE+1]; /* Master replication ID (if master). */
1534 int slave_listening_port; /* As configured with: REPLCONF listening-port */
1535 char *slave_addr; /* Optionally given by REPLCONF ip-address */
1536 int slave_capa; /* Slave capabilities: SLAVE_CAPA_* bitwise OR. */
1537 int slave_req; /* Slave requirements: SLAVE_REQ_* */
1538 uint64_t main_ch_client_id; /* The client id of this replica's main channel */
1539 multiState mstate; /* MULTI/EXEC state */
1540 blockingState bstate; /* blocking state */
1541 long long woff; /* Last write global replication offset. */
1542 list *watched_keys; /* Keys WATCHED for MULTI/EXEC CAS */
1543 dict *pubsub_channels; /* channels a client is interested in (SUBSCRIBE) */
1544 dict *pubsub_patterns; /* patterns a client is interested in (PSUBSCRIBE) */
1545 dict *pubsubshard_channels; /* shard level channels a client is interested in (SSUBSCRIBE) */
1546 sds peerid; /* Cached peer ID. */
1547 sds sockname; /* Cached connection target address. */
1548 listNode *client_list_node; /* list node in client list */
1549 listNode *io_thread_client_list_node; /* list node in io thread client list */
1550 listNode *postponed_list_node; /* list node within the postponed list */
1551 void *module_blocked_client; /* Pointer to the RedisModuleBlockedClient associated with this
1552 * client. This is set in case of module authentication before the
1553 * unblocked client is reprocessed to handle reply callbacks. */
1554 void *module_auth_ctx; /* Ongoing / attempted module based auth callback's ctx.
1555 * This is only tracked within the context of the command attempting
1556 * authentication. If not NULL, it means module auth is in progress. */
1557 RedisModuleUserChangedFunc auth_callback; /* Module callback to execute
1558 * when the authenticated user
1559 * changes. */
1560 void *auth_callback_privdata; /* Private data that is passed when the auth
1561 * changed callback is executed. Opaque for
1562 * Redis Core. */
1563 void *auth_module; /* The module that owns the callback, which is used
1564 * to disconnect the client if the module is
1565 * unloaded for cleanup. Opaque for Redis Core.*/
1566
1567 /* If this client is in tracking mode and this field is non zero,
1568 * invalidation messages for keys fetched by this client will be sent to
1569 * the specified client ID. */
1570 uint64_t client_tracking_redirection;
1571 rax *client_tracking_prefixes; /* A dictionary of prefixes we are already
1572 subscribed to in BCAST mode, in the
1573 context of client side caching. */
1574 /* In updateClientMemoryUsage() we track the memory usage of
1575 * each client and add it to the sum of all the clients of a given type,
1576 * however we need to remember what was the old contribution of each
1577 * client, and in which category the client was, in order to remove it
1578 * before adding it the new value. */
1579 size_t last_memory_usage;
1580 int last_memory_type;
1581
1582 listNode *mem_usage_bucket_node;
1583 clientMemUsageBucket *mem_usage_bucket;
1584
1585 listNode *ref_repl_buf_node; /* Referenced node of replication buffer blocks,
1586 * see the definition of replBufBlock. */
1587 size_t ref_block_pos; /* Access position of referenced buffer block,
1588 * i.e. the next offset to send. */
1589 listNode *io_curr_repl_node; /* Current node we are sending repl data from in
1590 * IO thread. */
1591 size_t io_curr_block_pos; /* Current position we are sending repl data from
1592 * in IO thread. */
1593 listNode *io_bound_repl_node;/* Bound node we are sending repl data from in
1594 * IO thread. */
1595 size_t io_bound_block_pos; /* Bound position we are sending repl data from
1596 * in IO thread. */
1597
1598 /* list node in clients_pending_write list */
1599 listNode clients_pending_write_node;
1600 /* list node in clients_with_pending_ref_reply list */
1601 listNode pending_ref_reply_node;
1602 /* Statistics and metrics */
1603 size_t net_input_bytes_curr_cmd; /* Total network input bytes read for the
1604 * execution of this client's current command. */
1605 size_t net_output_bytes_curr_cmd; /* Total network output bytes sent to this
1606 * client, by the current command. */
1607 /* Response buffer */
1608 size_t buf_peak; /* Peak used size of buffer in last 5 sec interval. */
1609 mstime_t buf_peak_last_reset_time; /* keeps the last time the buffer peak value was reset */
1610 size_t bufpos;
1611 size_t buf_usable_size; /* Usable size of buffer. */
1612 char *buf;
1613 uint8_t buf_encoded; /* True if c->buf content is encoded (e.g. for copy avoidance) */
1614 payloadHeader *last_header; /* Pointer to the last header in a buffer when using copy avoidance */
1615#ifdef LOG_REQ_RES
1616 clientReqResInfo reqres;
1617#endif
1618 unsigned long long net_input_bytes; /* Total network input bytes read from this client. */
1619 unsigned long long net_output_bytes; /* Total network output bytes sent to this client. */
1620 unsigned long long commands_processed; /* Total count of commands this client executed. */
1621 struct asmTask *task; /* Atomic slot migration task */
1622 char *node_id; /* Node ID to connect to for atomic slot migration */
1623
1624 redisAtomic int pending_read; /* Flag indicating an IO thread client residing
1625 * in main thread has received a read event. */
1626
1627 mstime_t last_ts_when_counted_as_active; /* Timestamp of last time this client was counted as active */
1628 size_t stat_total_read_events; /* Number of times readQueryFromClient() was called */
1629 size_t stat_avg_pipeline_length_sum; /* Sum of pipeline lengths for computing average */
1630 size_t stat_avg_pipeline_length_cnt; /* Count of pipeline length samples */
1631} client;
1632
1633typedef struct __attribute__((aligned(CACHE_LINE_SIZE))) {
1634 uint8_t id; /* The unique ID assigned, if IO_THREADS_MAX_NUM is more
1635 * than 256, we should also promote the data type. */
1636 pthread_t tid; /* Pthread ID */
1637 redisAtomic int paused; /* Paused status for the io thread. */
1638 redisAtomic int running; /* Running if true, main thread can send clients directly. */
1639 aeEventLoop *el; /* Main event loop of io thread. */
1640 list *pending_clients; /* List of clients with pending writes. */
1641 list *processing_clients; /* List of clients being processed. */
1642 eventNotifier *pending_clients_notifier; /* Used to wake up the loop when write should be performed. */
1643 pthread_mutex_t pending_clients_mutex; /* Mutex for pending write list */
1644 list *pending_clients_to_main_thread; /* Clients that are waiting to be executed by the main thread. */
1645 list *clients; /* IO thread managed clients. */
1646} IOThread;
1647
1648/* Context for streaming replDataBuf to database */
1649typedef struct replDataBufToDbCtx {
1650 void *privdata; /* Private data of context */
1651 client *client; /* Client to process commands */
1652 size_t applied_offset; /* Offset applied to the database */
1653 int (*should_continue)(void *ctx); /* Check if we should continue */
1654 void (*yield_callback)(void *ctx); /* Yield to the event loop */
1655} replDataBufToDbCtx;
1656
1657/* ACL information */
1658typedef struct aclInfo {
