1659 long long user_auth_failures; /* Auth failure counts on user level */
1660 long long invalid_cmd_accesses; /* Invalid command accesses that user doesn't have permission to */
1661 long long invalid_key_accesses; /* Invalid key accesses that user doesn't have permission to */
1662 long long invalid_channel_accesses; /* Invalid channel accesses that user doesn't have permission to */
1663 long long acl_access_denied_tls_cert; /* TLS clients with cert not matching any existing user. */
1664} aclInfo;
1665
1666struct saveparam {
1667 time_t seconds;
1668 int changes;
1669};
1670
1671struct moduleLoadQueueEntry {
1672 sds path;
1673 int argc;
1674 robj **argv;
1675};
1676
1677struct sentinelLoadQueueEntry {
1678 int argc;
1679 sds *argv;
1680 int linenum;
1681 sds line;
1682};
1683
1684struct sentinelConfig {
1685 list *pre_monitor_cfg;
1686 list *monitor_cfg;
1687 list *post_monitor_cfg;
1688};
1689
1690struct sharedObjectsStruct {
1691 robj *ok, *err, *emptybulk, *czero, *cone, *pong, *space,
1692 *queued, *null[4], *nullarray[4], *emptymap[4], *emptyset[4],
1693 *emptyarray, *wrongtypeerr, *nokeyerr, *syntaxerr, *sameobjecterr,
1694 *outofrangeerr, *noscripterr, *loadingerr,
1695 *slowevalerr, *slowscripterr, *slowmoduleerr, *bgsaveerr,
1696 *masterdownerr, *roslaveerr, *execaborterr, *noautherr, *noreplicaserr,
1697 *busykeyerr, *oomerr, *plus, *messagebulk, *pmessagebulk, *subscribebulk,
1698 *unsubscribebulk, *psubscribebulk, *punsubscribebulk, *del, *unlink,
1699 *rpop, *lpop, *lpush, *rpoplpush, *lmove, *blmove, *zpopmin, *zpopmax,
1700 *emptyscan, *multi, *exec, *left, *right, *hset, *srem, *xgroup, *xclaim,
1701 *script, *replconf, *eval, *persist, *set, *pexpireat, *pexpire,
1702 *hdel, *hpexpireat, *hpersist, *hsetex,
1703 *time, *pxat, *absttl, *retrycount, *force, *justid, *entriesread,
1704 *lastid, *ping, *setid, *keepttl, *load, *createconsumer, *fields,
1705 *getack, *special_asterick, *special_equals, *default_username, *redacted,
1706 *ssubscribebulk,*sunsubscribebulk, *smessagebulk,
1707 *select[PROTO_SHARED_SELECT_CMDS],
1708 *integers[OBJ_SHARED_INTEGERS],
1709 *mbulkhdr[OBJ_SHARED_BULKHDR_LEN], /* "*<value>\r\n" */
1710 *bulkhdr[OBJ_SHARED_BULKHDR_LEN], /* "$<value>\r\n" */
1711 *maphdr[OBJ_SHARED_BULKHDR_LEN], /* "%<value>\r\n" */
1712 *sethdr[OBJ_SHARED_BULKHDR_LEN]; /* "~<value>\r\n" */
1713 sds minstring, maxstring;
1714};
1715
1716/* ZSETs use a specialized version of Skiplists */
1717
1718/* Node info placed in level[0].span since it's unused at level 0 (static assert verified) */
1719typedef struct zskiplistNodeInfo {
1720 uint16_t sdsoffset; /* Offset from node start to sds data (after sds header) */
1721 uint8_t levels; /* Number of levels in this node (1-32) */
1722 uint8_t reserved;
1723} zskiplistNodeInfo;
1724
1725typedef struct zskiplistNode {
1726 double score;
1727 struct zskiplistNode *backward;
1728 struct zskiplistLevel {
1729 struct zskiplistNode *forward;
1730 /* Span is the number of elements between this node and the next node at this level.
1731 * At level 0, span is repurposed to store zskiplistNodeInfo for regular nodes, */
1732 unsigned long span;
1733 } level[];
1734 /* sds ele is embedded after level[] array (assist zslGetNodeElement(node) to access it) */
1735} zskiplistNode;
1736
1737typedef struct zskiplist {
1738 struct zskiplistNode *header, *tail;
1739 unsigned long length;
1740 int level;
1741 size_t alloc_size;
1742} zskiplist;
1743
1744typedef struct zset {
1745 dict *dict;
1746 zskiplist *zsl;
1747} zset;
1748
1749typedef struct clientBufferLimitsConfig {
1750 unsigned long long hard_limit_bytes;
1751 unsigned long long soft_limit_bytes;
1752 time_t soft_limit_seconds;
1753} clientBufferLimitsConfig;
1754
1755extern clientBufferLimitsConfig clientBufferLimitsDefaults[CLIENT_TYPE_OBUF_COUNT];
1756
1757/* The redisOp structure defines a Redis Operation, that is an instance of
1758 * a command with an argument vector, database ID, propagation target
1759 * (PROPAGATE_*), and command pointer.
1760 *
1761 * Currently only used to additionally propagate more commands to AOF/Replication
1762 * after the propagation of the executed command. */
1763typedef struct redisOp {
1764 robj **argv;
1765 int argc, dbid, target;
1766} redisOp;
1767
1768/* Defines an array of Redis operations. There is an API to add to this
1769 * structure in an easy way.
1770 *
1771 * int redisOpArrayAppend(redisOpArray *oa, int dbid, robj **argv, int argc, int target);
1772 * void redisOpArrayFree(redisOpArray *oa);
1773 */
1774typedef struct redisOpArray {
1775 redisOp *ops;
1776 int numops;
1777 int capacity;
1778} redisOpArray;
1779
1780/* This structure is returned by the getMemoryOverheadData() function in
1781 * order to return memory overhead information. */
1782struct redisMemOverhead {
1783 size_t peak_allocated;
1784 size_t total_allocated;
1785 size_t startup_allocated;
1786 size_t repl_backlog;
1787 size_t replica_fullsync_buffer;
1788 size_t clients_slaves;
1789 size_t clients_normal;
1790 size_t cluster_links;
1791 size_t aof_buffer;
1792 size_t eval_caches;
1793 size_t functions_caches;
1794 size_t script_vm;
1795 size_t overhead_total;
1796 size_t dataset;
1797 size_t total_keys;
1798 size_t bytes_per_key;
1799 float dataset_perc;
1800 float peak_perc;
1801 float total_frag;
1802 ssize_t total_frag_bytes;
1803 float allocator_frag;
1804 ssize_t allocator_frag_bytes;
1805 float allocator_rss;
1806 ssize_t allocator_rss_bytes;
1807 float rss_extra;
1808 ssize_t rss_extra_bytes;
1809 size_t num_dbs;
1810 size_t overhead_db_hashtable_lut;
1811 size_t overhead_db_hashtable_rehashing;
1812 unsigned long db_dict_rehashing_count;
1813 size_t asm_import_input_buffer;
1814 size_t asm_migrate_output_buffer;
1815 struct {
1816 size_t dbid;
1817 size_t overhead_ht_main;
1818 size_t overhead_ht_expires;
1819 } *db;
1820};
1821
1822/* Replication error behavior determines the replica behavior
1823 * when it receives an error over the replication stream. In
1824 * either case the error is logged. */
1825typedef enum {
1826 PROPAGATION_ERR_BEHAVIOR_IGNORE = 0,
1827 PROPAGATION_ERR_BEHAVIOR_PANIC,
1828 PROPAGATION_ERR_BEHAVIOR_PANIC_ON_REPLICAS
1829} replicationErrorBehavior;
1830
1831/* This structure can be optionally passed to RDB save/load functions in
1832 * order to implement additional functionalities, by storing and loading
1833 * metadata to the RDB file.
1834 *
1835 * For example, to use select a DB at load time, useful in
1836 * replication in order to make sure that chained slaves (slaves of slaves)
1837 * select the correct DB and are able to accept the stream coming from the
1838 * top-level master. */
1839typedef struct rdbSaveInfo {
1840 /* Used saving and loading. */
1841 int repl_stream_db; /* DB to select in server.master client. */
1842
1843 /* Used only loading. */
1844 int repl_id_is_set; /* True if repl_id field is set. */
1845 char repl_id[CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE+1]; /* Replication ID. */
1846 long long repl_offset; /* Replication offset. */
1847} rdbSaveInfo;
1848
1849#define RDB_SAVE_INFO_INIT {-1,0,"0000000000000000000000000000000000000000",-1}
1850
1851struct malloc_stats {
1852 size_t zmalloc_used;
1853 size_t process_rss;
1854 size_t allocator_allocated;
1855 size_t allocator_active;
1856 size_t allocator_resident;
1857 size_t allocator_muzzy;
1858 size_t allocator_frag_smallbins_bytes;
1859 size_t lua_allocator_allocated;
1860 size_t lua_allocator_active;
1861 size_t lua_allocator_resident;
1862 size_t lua_allocator_frag_smallbins_bytes;
1863};
1864
1865/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1866 * TLS Context Configuration
1867 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
1868
1869typedef struct redisTLSContextConfig {
1870 char *cert_file; /* Server side and optionally client side cert file name */
1871 char *key_file; /* Private key filename for cert_file */
1872 char *key_file_pass; /* Optional password for key_file */
1873 char *client_cert_file; /* Certificate to use as a client; if none, use cert_file */
1874 char *client_key_file; /* Private key filename for client_cert_file */
1875 char *client_key_file_pass; /* Optional password for client_key_file */
1876 int client_auth_user; /* Field to be used for automatic TLS authentication based on client TLS certificate */
1877 char *dh_params_file;
1878 char *ca_cert_file;
1879 char *ca_cert_dir;
1880 char *protocols;
1881 char *ciphers;
1882 char *ciphersuites;
1883 int prefer_server_ciphers;
1884 int session_caching;
1885 int session_cache_size;
1886 int session_cache_timeout;
1887} redisTLSContextConfig;
1888
1889/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1890 * AOF manifest definition
1891 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
1892typedef enum {
1893 AOF_FILE_TYPE_BASE = 'b', /* BASE file */
1894 AOF_FILE_TYPE_HIST = 'h', /* HISTORY file */
1895 AOF_FILE_TYPE_INCR = 'i', /* INCR file */
1896} aof_file_type;
1897
1898typedef struct {
1899 sds file_name; /* file name */
1900 long long file_seq; /* file sequence */
1901 aof_file_type file_type; /* file type */
1902 long long start_offset; /* the start replication offset of the file */
1903 long long end_offset; /* the end replication offset of the file */
1904} aofInfo;
1905
1906typedef struct {
1907 aofInfo *base_aof_info; /* BASE file information. NULL if there is no BASE file. */
1908 list *incr_aof_list; /* INCR AOFs list. We may have multiple INCR AOF when rewrite fails. */
1909 list *history_aof_list; /* HISTORY AOF list. When the AOFRW success, The aofInfo contained in
1910 `base_aof_info` and `incr_aof_list` will be moved to this list. We
1911 will delete these AOF files when AOFRW finish. */
1912 long long curr_base_file_seq; /* The sequence number used by the current BASE file. */
1913 long long curr_incr_file_seq; /* The sequence number used by the current INCR file. */
1914 int dirty; /* 1 Indicates that the aofManifest in the memory is inconsistent with
1915 disk, we need to persist it immediately. */
1916} aofManifest;
1917
1918/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
1919 * Global server state
1920 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
1921
1922/* AIX defines hz to __hz, we don't use this define and in order to allow
1923 * Redis build on AIX we need to undef it. */
1924#ifdef _AIX
1925#undef hz
1926#endif
1927
1928#define CHILD_TYPE_NONE 0
1929#define CHILD_TYPE_RDB 1
1930#define CHILD_TYPE_AOF 2
1931#define CHILD_TYPE_LDB 3
1932#define CHILD_TYPE_MODULE 4
1933
1934typedef enum childInfoType {
1935 CHILD_INFO_TYPE_CURRENT_INFO,
1936 CHILD_INFO_TYPE_AOF_COW_SIZE,
1937 CHILD_INFO_TYPE_RDB_COW_SIZE,
1938 CHILD_INFO_TYPE_MODULE_COW_SIZE
1939} childInfoType;
1940
1941typedef struct hotkeyStats hotkeyStats;
1942
1943struct redisServer {
1944 /* General */
1945 pid_t pid; /* Main process pid. */
1946 pthread_t main_thread_id; /* Main thread id */
1947 char *configfile; /* Absolute config file path, or NULL */
1948 char *executable; /* Absolute executable file path. */
1949 char **exec_argv; /* Executable argv vector (copy). */
1950 int dynamic_hz; /* Change hz value depending on # of clients. */
1951 int config_hz; /* Configured HZ value. May be different than
1952 the actual 'hz' field value if dynamic-hz
1953 is enabled. */
1954 mode_t umask; /* The umask value of the process on startup */
1955 int hz; /* serverCron() calls frequency in hertz */
1956 int in_fork_child; /* indication that this is a fork child */
1957 redisDb *db;
1958 dict *commands; /* Command table */
1959 dict *orig_commands; /* Command table before command renaming. */
1960 aeEventLoop *el;
1961 rax *errors; /* Errors table */
1962 int errors_enabled; /* If true, errorstats is enabled, and we will add new errors. */
1963 unsigned int lruclock; /* Clock for LRU eviction */
1964 redisAtomic int shutdown_asap; /* Shutdown ordered by signal handler. */
1965 redisAtomic int crashing; /* Server is crashing report. */
1966 mstime_t shutdown_mstime; /* Timestamp to limit graceful shutdown. */
1967 redisAtomic int last_sig_received; /* Indicates the last SIGNAL received, if any (e.g., SIGINT or SIGTERM). */
1968 int shutdown_flags; /* Flags passed to prepareForShutdown(). */
1969 int activerehashing; /* Incremental rehash in serverCron() */
1970 int active_defrag_running; /* Active defragmentation running (holds current scan aggressiveness) */
1971 char *pidfile; /* PID file path */
1972 int arch_bits; /* 32 or 64 depending on sizeof(long) */
1973 int cronloops; /* Number of times the cron function run */
1974 char runid[CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE+1]; /* ID always different at every exec. */
1975 int sentinel_mode; /* True if this instance is a Sentinel. */
1976 size_t initial_memory_usage; /* Bytes used after initialization. */
1977 int always_show_logo; /* Show logo even for non-stdout logging. */
1978 int in_exec; /* Are we inside EXEC? */
1979 int busy_module_yield_flags; /* Are we inside a busy module? (triggered by RM_Yield). see BUSY_MODULE_YIELD_ flags. */
1980 const char *busy_module_yield_reply; /* When non-null, we are inside RM_Yield. */
1981 char *ignore_warnings; /* Config: warnings that should be ignored. */
1982 int client_pause_in_transaction; /* Was a client pause executed during this Exec? */
1983 int thp_enabled; /* If true, THP is enabled. */
1984 size_t page_size; /* The page size of OS. */
1985 redisAtomic int running; /* Running if true, IO threads can send clients without notification */
1986 /* Modules */
1987 dict *moduleapi; /* Exported core APIs dictionary for modules. */
1988 dict *sharedapi; /* Like moduleapi but containing the APIs that
1989 modules share with each other. */
1990 dict *module_configs_queue; /* Unmapped configs are queued here, assumed to be module config. Applied after modules are loaded during startup or arguments to loadex. */
1991 list *loadmodule_queue; /* List of modules to load at startup. */
1992 int module_pipe[2]; /* Pipe used to awake the event loop by module threads. */
1993 pid_t child_pid; /* PID of current child */
1994 int child_type; /* Type of current child */
1995 redisAtomic int module_gil_acquring; /* Indicates whether the GIL is being acquiring by the main thread. */
1996 /* Networking */
1997 int port; /* TCP listening port */
1998 int tls_port; /* TLS listening port */
1999 int tcp_backlog; /* TCP listen() backlog */
2000 char *bindaddr[CONFIG_BINDADDR_MAX]; /* Addresses we should bind to */
2001 int bindaddr_count; /* Number of addresses in server.bindaddr[] */
2002 char *bind_source_addr; /* Source address to bind on for outgoing connections */
2003 char *unixsocket; /* UNIX socket path */
2004 unsigned int unixsocketperm; /* UNIX socket permission (see mode_t) */
2005 connListener listeners[CONN_TYPE_MAX]; /* TCP/Unix/TLS even more types */
2006 uint32_t socket_mark_id; /* ID for listen socket marking */
2007 connListener clistener; /* Cluster bus listener */
2008 list *clients; /* List of active clients */
2009 list *clients_to_close; /* Clients to close asynchronously */
2010 list *clients_pending_write; /* There is to write or install handler. */
2011 list *clients_pending_read; /* Client has pending read socket buffers. */
2012 list *clients_with_pending_ref_reply; /* Clients with referenced reply objects. */
2013 list *slaves, *monitors; /* List of slaves and MONITORs */
2014 client *current_client; /* The client that triggered the command execution (External or AOF). */
2015 client *executing_client; /* The client executing the current command (possibly script or module). */
2016
2017#ifdef LOG_REQ_RES
2018 char *req_res_logfile; /* Path of log file for logging all requests and their replies. If NULL, no logging will be performed */
2019 unsigned int client_default_resp;
2020#endif
2021
2022 /* Stuff for client mem eviction */
2023 clientMemUsageBucket* client_mem_usage_buckets;
2024
2025 rax *clients_timeout_table; /* Radix tree for blocked clients timeouts. */
2026 int execution_nesting; /* Execution nesting level.
2027 * e.g. call(), async module stuff (timers, events, etc.),
2028 * cron stuff (active expire, eviction) */
2029 rax *clients_index; /* Active clients dictionary by client ID. */
2030 uint32_t paused_actions; /* Bitmask of actions that are currently paused */
2031 list *postponed_clients; /* List of postponed clients */
2032 pause_event client_pause_per_purpose[NUM_PAUSE_PURPOSES];
2033 char neterr[ANET_ERR_LEN]; /* Error buffer for anet.c */
2034 dict *migrate_cached_sockets;/* MIGRATE cached sockets */
2035 redisAtomic uint64_t next_client_id; /* Next client unique ID. Incremental. */
2036 int protected_mode; /* Don't accept external connections. */
2037 int io_threads_num; /* Number of IO threads to use. */
2038 int io_threads_clients_num[IO_THREADS_MAX_NUM]; /* Number of clients assigned to each IO thread. */
2039 int io_threads_do_reads; /* Read and parse from IO threads? */
2040 int io_threads_active; /* Is IO threads currently active? */
2041 pendingCommandPool cmd_pool; /* Shared pool for reusing pendingCommand,
2042 * only when IO threads disabled */
2043 int prefetch_batch_max_size;/* Maximum number of keys to prefetch in a single batch */
2044 long long events_processed_while_blocked; /* processEventsWhileBlocked() */
2045 int enable_protected_configs; /* Enable the modification of protected configs, see PROTECTED_ACTION_ALLOWED_* */
2046 int enable_debug_cmd; /* Enable DEBUG commands, see PROTECTED_ACTION_ALLOWED_* */
2047 int enable_module_cmd; /* Enable MODULE commands, see PROTECTED_ACTION_ALLOWED_* */
2048
2049 /* RDB / AOF loading information */
2050 volatile sig_atomic_t loading; /* We are loading data from disk if true */
2051 volatile sig_atomic_t async_loading; /* We are loading data without blocking the db being served */
2052 off_t loading_total_bytes;
2053 off_t loading_rdb_used_mem;
2054 off_t loading_loaded_bytes;
2055 time_t loading_start_time;
2056 off_t loading_process_events_interval_bytes;
2057 int loading_skip_checksum; /* Skip checksum verification during diskless loading */
2058 /* Fields used only for stats */
2059 time_t stat_starttime; /* Server start time */
2060 long long stat_numcommands; /* Number of processed commands */
2061 long long stat_numconnections; /* Number of connections received */
2062 long long stat_expiredkeys; /* Number of expired keys */
2063 long long stat_expiredkeys_active; /* Number of expired keys by active expire */
2064 long long stat_expired_subkeys; /* Number of expired subkeys (Currently only hash-fields) */
2065 long long stat_expired_subkeys_active; /* Number of expired subkeys by active expire */
2066 double stat_expired_stale_perc; /* Percentage of keys probably expired */
2067 long long stat_expired_time_cap_reached_count; /* Early expire cycle stops.*/
2068 long long stat_expire_cycle_time_used; /* Cumulative microseconds used. */
2069 long long stat_evictedkeys; /* Number of evicted keys (maxmemory) */
2070 long long stat_evictedclients; /* Number of evicted clients */
2071 long long stat_evictedscripts; /* Number of evicted lua scripts. */
2072 long long stat_total_eviction_exceeded_time; /* Total time over the memory limit, unit us */
2073 monotime stat_last_eviction_exceeded_time; /* Timestamp of current eviction start, unit us */
2074 long long stat_keyspace_hits; /* Number of successful lookups of keys */
2075 long long stat_keyspace_misses; /* Number of failed lookups of keys */
2076 long long stat_active_defrag_hits; /* number of allocations moved */
2077 long long stat_active_defrag_misses; /* number of allocations scanned but not moved */
2078 long long stat_active_defrag_key_hits; /* number of keys with moved allocations */
2079 long long stat_active_defrag_key_misses;/* number of keys scanned and not moved */
2080 long long stat_active_defrag_scanned; /* number of dictEntries scanned */
2081 long long stat_total_active_defrag_time; /* Total time memory fragmentation over the limit, unit us */
2082 monotime stat_last_active_defrag_time; /* Timestamp of current active defrag start */
2083 size_t stat_peak_memory; /* Max used memory record */
2084 time_t stat_peak_memory_time; /* Time when stat_peak_memory was recorded */
2085 long long stat_aof_rewrites; /* number of aof file rewrites performed */
2086 long long stat_aofrw_consecutive_failures; /* The number of consecutive failures of aofrw */
2087 long long stat_rdb_saves; /* number of rdb saves performed */
2088 long long stat_rdb_consecutive_failures; /* The number of consecutive failures of rdb saves */
2089 long long stat_fork_time; /* Time needed to perform latest fork() */
2090 double stat_fork_rate; /* Fork rate in GB/sec. */
2091 long long stat_total_forks; /* Total count of fork. */
2092 long long stat_rejected_conn; /* Clients rejected because of maxclients */
2093 long long stat_sync_full; /* Number of full resyncs with slaves. */
2094 long long stat_sync_partial_ok; /* Number of accepted PSYNC requests. */
2095 long long stat_sync_partial_err;/* Number of unaccepted PSYNC requests. */
2096 list *slowlog; /* SLOWLOG list of commands */
2097 long long slowlog_entry_id; /* SLOWLOG current entry ID */
2098 long long slowlog_log_slower_than; /* SLOWLOG time limit (to get logged) */
2099 unsigned long slowlog_max_len; /* SLOWLOG max number of items logged */
2100 long long stat_slowlog_count; /* Total slowlog entries ever pushed */
2101 long long stat_slowlog_time_us_sum; /* Sum of all slowlog entry durations (usec) */
2102 long long stat_slowlog_time_us_max; /* Max slowlog entry duration (usec) */
2103 struct malloc_stats cron_malloc_stats; /* sampled in serverCron(). */
2104 redisAtomic long long stat_net_input_bytes; /* Bytes read from network. */
2105 redisAtomic long long stat_net_output_bytes; /* Bytes written to network. */
2106 redisAtomic long long stat_net_repl_input_bytes; /* Bytes read during replication, added to stat_net_input_bytes in 'info'. */
2107 redisAtomic long long stat_net_repl_output_bytes; /* Bytes written during replication, added to stat_net_output_bytes in 'info'. */
2108 size_t stat_current_cow_peak; /* Peak size of copy on write bytes. */
2109 size_t stat_current_cow_bytes; /* Copy on write bytes while child is active. */
2110 monotime stat_current_cow_updated; /* Last update time of stat_current_cow_bytes */
2111 size_t stat_current_save_keys_processed; /* Processed keys while child is active. */
2112 size_t stat_current_save_keys_total; /* Number of keys when child started. */
2113 size_t stat_rdb_cow_bytes; /* Copy on write bytes during RDB saving. */
2114 size_t stat_aof_cow_bytes; /* Copy on write bytes during AOF rewrite. */
2115 size_t stat_module_cow_bytes; /* Copy on write bytes during module fork. */
2116 double stat_module_progress; /* Module save progress. */
2117 size_t stat_clients_type_memory[CLIENT_TYPE_COUNT];/* Mem usage by type */
2118 size_t stat_cluster_links_memory; /* Mem usage by cluster links */
2119 long long stat_unexpected_error_replies; /* Number of unexpected (aof-loading, replica to master, etc.) error replies */
2120 long long stat_total_error_replies; /* Total number of issued error replies ( command + rejected errors ) */
2121 long long stat_dump_payload_sanitizations; /* Number deep dump payloads integrity validations. */
2122 redisAtomic long long stat_io_reads_processed[IO_THREADS_MAX_NUM]; /* Number of read events processed by IO / Main threads */
2123 redisAtomic long long stat_io_writes_processed[IO_THREADS_MAX_NUM]; /* Number of write events processed by IO / Main threads */
2124 redisAtomic long long stat_client_qbuf_limit_disconnections; /* Total number of clients reached query buf length limit */
2125 long long stat_client_outbuf_limit_disconnections; /* Total number of clients reached output buf length limit */
2126 long long stat_cluster_incompatible_ops; /* Number of operations that are incompatible with cluster mode */
2127 long long stat_total_prefetch_entries; /* Total number of prefetched dict entries */
2128 long long stat_total_prefetch_batches; /* Total number of prefetched batches */
2129 redisAtomic long long stat_avg_pipeline_length_sum; /* Sum of pipeline lengths for computing average */
2130 redisAtomic long long stat_avg_pipeline_length_cnt; /* Count of pipeline length samples */
2131 redisAtomic long long stat_total_client_process_input_buff_events; /* Number of times processInputBuffer() was called */
2132 size_t stat_eventloop_cycles_with_clients_input_buff_processing; /* Event loop cycles with client input buffer processing */
2133 /* The following two are used to track instantaneous metrics, like
2134 * number of operations per second, network traffic. */
2135 struct {
2136 long long last_sample_base; /* The divisor of last sample window */
2137 long long last_sample_value; /* The dividend of last sample window */
2138 long long samples[STATS_METRIC_SAMPLES];
2139 int idx;
2140 } inst_metric[STATS_METRIC_COUNT];
2141 long long stat_reply_buffer_shrinks; /* Total number of output buffer shrinks */
2142 long long stat_reply_buffer_expands; /* Total number of output buffer expands */
2143 monotime el_start;
2144 /* The following two are used to record the max number of commands executed in one eventloop.
2145 * Note that commands in transactions are also counted. */
2146 long long el_cmd_cnt_start;
2147 long long el_cmd_cnt_max;
2148 /* The sum of active-expire, active-defrag and all other tasks done by cron and beforeSleep,
2149 but excluding read, write and AOF, which are counted by other sets of metrics. */
2150 monotime el_cron_duration;
2151 durationStats duration_stats[EL_DURATION_TYPE_NUM];
2152
2153 /* Hotkey tracking */
2154 hotkeyStats *hotkeys;
2155
2156 /* Configuration */
2157 int verbosity; /* Loglevel in redis.conf */
2158 int hide_user_data_from_log; /* In the event of an assertion failure, hide command arguments from the operator */
2159 int maxidletime; /* Client timeout in seconds */
2160 int tcpkeepalive; /* Set SO_KEEPALIVE if non-zero. */
2161 int active_expire_enabled; /* Can be disabled for testing purposes. */
2162 int active_expire_effort; /* From 1 (default) to 10, active effort. */
2163 int allow_access_expired; /* If > 0, allow access to logically expired keys */
2164 int allow_access_trimmed; /* If > 0, allow access to logically trimmed keys */
2165 int active_defrag_enabled;
2166 int sanitize_dump_payload; /* Enables deep sanitization for ziplist and listpack in RDB and RESTORE. */
2167 int skip_checksum_validation; /* Disable checksum validation for RDB and RESTORE payload. */
2168 int jemalloc_bg_thread; /* Enable jemalloc background thread */
2169 int active_defrag_configuration_changed; /* defrag configuration has been changed and need to reconsider
2170 * active_defrag_running in computeDefragCycles. */
2171 size_t active_defrag_ignore_bytes; /* minimum amount of fragmentation waste to start active defrag */
2172 int active_defrag_threshold_lower; /* minimum percentage of fragmentation to start active defrag */
2173 int active_defrag_threshold_upper; /* maximum percentage of fragmentation at which we use maximum effort */
2174 int active_defrag_cycle_min; /* minimal effort for defrag in CPU percentage */
2175 int active_defrag_cycle_max; /* maximal effort for defrag in CPU percentage */
2176 unsigned long active_defrag_max_scan_fields; /* maximum number of fields of set/hash/zset/list to process from within the main dict scan */
2177 size_t client_max_querybuf_len; /* Limit for client query buffer length */
2178 int lookahead; /* how many commands in each client pipeline to decode and prefetch */
2179 int dbnum; /* Total number of configured DBs */
2180 int supervised; /* 1 if supervised, 0 otherwise. */
2181 int supervised_mode; /* See SUPERVISED_* */
2182 int daemonize; /* True if running as a daemon */
2183 int set_proc_title; /* True if change proc title */
2184 char *proc_title_template; /* Process title template format */
2185 clientBufferLimitsConfig client_obuf_limits[CLIENT_TYPE_OBUF_COUNT];
2186 int pause_cron; /* Don't run cron tasks (debug) */
2187 int dict_resizing; /* Whether to allow main dict and expired dict to be resized (debug) */
2188 int latency_tracking_enabled; /* 1 if extended latency tracking is enabled, 0 otherwise. */
2189 double *latency_tracking_info_percentiles; /* Extended latency tracking info output percentile list configuration. */
2190 int latency_tracking_info_percentiles_len;
2191 int memory_tracking_enabled; /* Account used memory per slot */
2192 unsigned int max_new_tls_conns_per_cycle; /* The maximum number of tls connections that will be accepted during each invocation of the event loop. */
2193 unsigned int max_new_conns_per_cycle; /* The maximum number of tcp connections that will be accepted during each invocation of the event loop. */
2194 int cluster_compatibility_sample_ratio; /* Sampling ratio for cluster mode incompatible commands. */
2195 int lazyexpire_nested_arbitrary_keys; /* If disabled, avoid lazy-expire from commands that touch arbitrary keys (SCAN/RANDOMKEY) within transactions */
2196
2197 /* AOF persistence */
2198 int aof_enabled; /* AOF configuration */
2199 int aof_state; /* AOF_(ON|OFF|WAIT_REWRITE) */
2200 int aof_fsync; /* Kind of fsync() policy */
2201 char *aof_filename; /* Basename of the AOF file and manifest file */
2202 char *aof_dirname; /* Name of the AOF directory */
2203 int aof_no_fsync_on_rewrite; /* Don't fsync if a rewrite is in prog. */
2204 int aof_rewrite_perc; /* Rewrite AOF if % growth is > M and... */
2205 off_t aof_rewrite_min_size; /* the AOF file is at least N bytes. */
2206 off_t aof_rewrite_base_size; /* AOF size on latest startup or rewrite. */
2207 off_t aof_current_size; /* AOF current size (Including BASE + INCRs). */
2208 off_t aof_last_incr_size; /* The size of the latest incr AOF. */
2209 off_t aof_last_incr_fsync_offset; /* AOF offset which is already requested to be synced to disk.
2210 * Compare with the aof_last_incr_size. */
2211 int aof_flush_sleep; /* Micros to sleep before flush. (used by tests) */
2212 int aof_rewrite_scheduled; /* Rewrite once BGSAVE terminates. */
2213 sds aof_buf; /* AOF buffer, written before entering the event loop */
2214 int aof_fd; /* File descriptor of currently selected AOF file */
2215 int aof_selected_db; /* Currently selected DB in AOF */
2216 mstime_t aof_flush_postponed_start; /* mstime of postponed AOF flush */
2217 mstime_t aof_last_fsync; /* mstime of last fsync() */
2218 time_t aof_rewrite_time_last; /* Time used by last AOF rewrite run. */
2219 time_t aof_rewrite_time_start; /* Current AOF rewrite start time. */
2220 time_t aof_cur_timestamp; /* Current record timestamp in AOF */
2221 int aof_timestamp_enabled; /* Enable record timestamp in AOF */
2222 int aof_lastbgrewrite_status; /* C_OK or C_ERR */
2223 unsigned long aof_delayed_fsync; /* delayed AOF fsync() counter */
2224 int aof_rewrite_incremental_fsync;/* fsync incrementally while aof rewriting? */
2225 int rdb_save_incremental_fsync; /* fsync incrementally while rdb saving? */
2226 int aof_last_write_status; /* C_OK or C_ERR */
2227 int aof_last_write_errno; /* Valid if aof write/fsync status is ERR */
2228 int aof_load_truncated; /* Don't stop on unexpected AOF EOF. */
2229 off_t aof_load_corrupt_tail_max_size; /* The max size of broken AOF tail than can be ignored. */
2230 int aof_use_rdb_preamble; /* Specify base AOF to use RDB encoding on AOF rewrites. */
2231 redisAtomic int aof_bio_fsync_status; /* Status of AOF fsync in bio job. */
2232 redisAtomic int aof_bio_fsync_errno; /* Errno of AOF fsync in bio job. */
2233 aofManifest *aof_manifest; /* Used to track AOFs. */
2234 int aof_disable_auto_gc; /* If disable automatically deleting HISTORY type AOFs?
2235 default no. (for testings). */
2236
2237 /* RDB persistence */
2238 long long dirty; /* Changes to DB from the last save */
2239 long long dirty_before_bgsave; /* Used to restore dirty on failed BGSAVE */
2240 long long rdb_last_load_keys_expired; /* number of expired keys when loading RDB */
2241 long long rdb_last_load_keys_loaded; /* number of loaded keys when loading RDB */
2242 int bgsave_aborted; /* Set when killing a child, to treat it as aborted even if it succeeds. */
2243 struct saveparam *saveparams; /* Save points array for RDB */
2244 int saveparamslen; /* Number of saving points */
2245 char *rdb_filename; /* Name of RDB file */
2246 int rdb_compression; /* Use compression in RDB? */
2247 int rdb_checksum; /* Use RDB checksum? */
2248 int rdb_del_sync_files; /* Remove RDB files used only for SYNC if
2249 the instance does not use persistence. */
2250 time_t lastsave; /* Unix time of last successful save */
2251 time_t lastbgsave_try; /* Unix time of last attempted bgsave */
2252 time_t rdb_save_time_last; /* Time used by last RDB save run. */
2253 time_t rdb_save_time_start; /* Current RDB save start time. */
2254 int rdb_bgsave_scheduled; /* BGSAVE when possible if true. */
2255 int rdb_child_type; /* Type of save by active child. */
2256 int lastbgsave_status; /* C_OK or C_ERR */
2257 int stop_writes_on_bgsave_err; /* Don't allow writes if can't BGSAVE */
2258 int rdb_pipe_read; /* RDB pipe used to transfer the rdb data */
2259 /* to the parent process in diskless repl. */
2260 int rdb_child_exit_pipe; /* Used by the diskless parent allow child exit. */
2261 connection **rdb_pipe_conns; /* Connections which are currently the */
2262 int rdb_pipe_numconns; /* target of diskless rdb fork child. */
2263 int rdb_pipe_numconns_writing; /* Number of rdb conns with pending writes. */
2264 char *rdb_pipe_buff; /* In diskless replication, this buffer holds data */
2265 int rdb_pipe_bufflen; /* that was read from the rdb pipe. */
2266 int rdb_key_save_delay; /* Delay in microseconds between keys while
2267 * writing aof or rdb. (for testings). negative
2268 * value means fractions of microseconds (on average). */
2269 int key_load_delay; /* Delay in microseconds between keys while
2270 * loading aof or rdb. (for testings). negative
2271 * value means fractions of microseconds (on average). */
2272 /* Pipe and data structures for child -> parent info sharing. */
2273 int child_info_pipe[2]; /* Pipe used to write the child_info_data. */
2274 int child_info_nread; /* Num of bytes of the last read from pipe */
2275 /* Propagation of commands in AOF / replication */
2276 redisOpArray also_propagate; /* Additional command to propagate. */
2277 int replication_allowed; /* Are we allowed to replicate? */
2278 /* Logging */
2279 char *logfile; /* Path of log file */
2280 int syslog_enabled; /* Is syslog enabled? */
2281 char *syslog_ident; /* Syslog ident */
2282 int syslog_facility; /* Syslog facility */
2283 int crashlog_enabled; /* Enable signal handler for crashlog.
2284 * disable for clean core dumps. */
2285 int memcheck_enabled; /* Enable memory check on crash. */
2286 int use_exit_on_panic; /* Use exit() on panic and assert rather than
2287 * abort(). useful for Valgrind. */
2288 /* Shutdown */
2289 int shutdown_timeout; /* Graceful shutdown time limit in seconds. */
2290 int shutdown_on_sigint; /* Shutdown flags configured for SIGINT. */
2291 int shutdown_on_sigterm; /* Shutdown flags configured for SIGTERM. */
2292
2293 /* Replication (master) */
2294 char replid[CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE+1]; /* My current replication ID. */
2295 char replid2[CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE+1]; /* replid inherited from master*/
2296 long long master_repl_offset; /* My current replication offset */
2297 long long second_replid_offset; /* Accept offsets up to this for replid2. */
2298 redisAtomic long long fsynced_reploff_pending;/* Largest replication offset to
2299 * potentially have been fsynced, applied to
2300 fsynced_reploff only when AOF state is AOF_ON
2301 (not during the initial rewrite) */
2302 long long fsynced_reploff; /* Largest replication offset that has been confirmed to be fsynced */
2303 int slaveseldb; /* Last SELECTed DB in replication output */
2304 int repl_ping_slave_period; /* Master pings the slave every N seconds */
2305 replBacklog *repl_backlog; /* Replication backlog for partial syncs */
2306 long long repl_backlog_size; /* Backlog circular buffer size */
2307 long long repl_full_sync_buffer_limit; /* Accumulated repl data limit during rdb channel replication */
2308 replDataBuf repl_full_sync_buffer; /* Accumulated replication data for rdb channel replication */
2309 time_t repl_backlog_time_limit; /* Time without slaves after the backlog
2310 gets released. */
2311 time_t repl_no_slaves_since; /* We have no slaves since that time.
2312 Only valid if server.slaves len is 0. */
2313 int repl_min_slaves_to_write; /* Min number of slaves to write. */
2314 int repl_min_slaves_max_lag; /* Max lag of <count> slaves to write. */
2315 int repl_good_slaves_count; /* Number of slaves with lag <= max_lag. */
2316 int repl_diskless_sync; /* Master send RDB to slaves sockets directly. */
2317 int repl_diskless_load; /* Slave parse RDB directly from the socket.
2318 * see REPL_DISKLESS_LOAD_* enum */
2319 int repl_diskless_sync_delay; /* Delay to start a diskless repl BGSAVE. */
2320 int repl_diskless_sync_max_replicas;/* Max replicas for diskless repl BGSAVE
2321 * delay (start sooner if they all connect). */
2322 int repl_rdb_channel; /* Config used to determine if the replica should
2323 * use rdb channel replication for full syncs. */
2324 int repl_debug_pause; /* Debug config to force the main process to pause. */
2325 size_t repl_buffer_mem; /* The memory of replication buffer. */
2326 list *repl_buffer_blocks; /* Replication buffers blocks list
2327 * (serving replica clients and repl backlog) */
2328 time_t repl_stream_lastio; /* Unix time of the latest sending replication stream. */
2329 /* Replication (slave) */
2330 char *masteruser; /* AUTH with this user and masterauth with master */
2331 sds masterauth; /* AUTH with this password with master */
2332 char *masterhost; /* Hostname of master */
2333 int masterport; /* Port of master */
2334 int repl_timeout; /* Timeout after N seconds of master idle */
2335 client *master; /* Client that is master for this slave */
2336 client *cached_master; /* Cached master to be reused for PSYNC. */
2337 int repl_syncio_timeout; /* Timeout for synchronous I/O calls */
2338 int repl_state; /* Replication status if the instance is a slave */
2339 int repl_rdb_ch_state; /* State of the replica's rdb channel during rdb channel replication */
2340 int repl_main_ch_state; /* State of the replica's main channel during rdb channel replication */
2341 uint64_t repl_num_master_disconnection; /* Number of master connection was disconnected */
2342 uint64_t repl_main_ch_client_id; /* Main channel client id received in +RDBCHANNELSYNC reply. */
2343 off_t repl_transfer_size; /* Size of RDB to read from master during sync. */
2344 off_t repl_transfer_read; /* Amount of RDB read from master during sync. */
2345 off_t repl_transfer_last_fsync_off; /* Offset when we fsync-ed last time. */
2346 connection *repl_transfer_s; /* Slave -> Master SYNC connection */
2347 connection *repl_rdb_transfer_s; /* Slave -> Master FULL SYNC connection (RDB download) */
2348 int repl_transfer_fd; /* Slave -> Master SYNC temp file descriptor */
2349 char *repl_transfer_tmpfile; /* Slave-> master SYNC temp file name */
2350 time_t repl_transfer_lastio; /* Unix time of the latest read, for timeout */
2351 int repl_serve_stale_data; /* Serve stale data when link is down? */
2352 int repl_slave_ro; /* Slave is read only? */
2353 int repl_slave_ignore_maxmemory; /* If true slaves do not evict. */
2354 time_t repl_down_since; /* Unix time at which link with master went down */
2355 time_t repl_up_since; /* Unix time that master link is fully up and healthy */
2356 int repl_disable_tcp_nodelay; /* Disable TCP_NODELAY after SYNC? */
2357 int slave_priority; /* Reported in INFO and used by Sentinel. */
2358 int replica_announced; /* If true, replica is announced by Sentinel */
2359 int slave_announce_port; /* Give the master this listening port. */
2360 char *slave_announce_ip; /* Give the master this ip address. */
2361 int propagation_error_behavior; /* Configures the behavior of the replica
2362 * when it receives an error on the replication stream */
2363 int repl_ignore_disk_write_error; /* Configures whether replicas panic when unable to
2364 * persist writes to AOF. */
2365 /* The following two fields is where we store master PSYNC replid/offset
2366 * while the PSYNC is in progress. At the end we'll copy the fields into
2367 * the server->master client structure. */
2368 char master_replid[CONFIG_RUN_ID_SIZE+1]; /* Master PSYNC runid. */
2369 long long master_initial_offset; /* Master PSYNC offset. */
2370 int repl_slave_lazy_flush; /* Lazy FLUSHALL before loading DB? */
2371 /* Synchronous replication. */
2372 list *clients_waiting_acks; /* Clients waiting in WAIT or WAITAOF. */
2373 int get_ack_from_slaves; /* If true we send REPLCONF GETACK. */
2374 long long repl_current_sync_attempts; /* Number of times in current configuration, the replica attempted to sync since the last success. */
2375 long long repl_total_sync_attempts; /* Number of times in current configuration, the replica attempted to sync to a master */
2376 time_t repl_disconnect_start_time; /* Unix time that master disconnection start */
2377 time_t repl_total_disconnect_time; /* The total cumulative time we've been disconnected as a replica, visible when the link is up too. */
2378 /* Limits */
2379 unsigned int maxclients; /* Max number of simultaneous clients */
2380 unsigned long long maxmemory; /* Max number of memory bytes to use */
2381 ssize_t maxmemory_clients; /* Memory limit for total client buffers */
2382 int maxmemory_policy; /* Policy for key eviction */
2383 int maxmemory_samples; /* Precision of random sampling */
2384 int maxmemory_eviction_tenacity;/* Aggressiveness of eviction processing */
2385 int lfu_log_factor; /* LFU logarithmic counter factor. */
2386 int lfu_decay_time; /* LFU counter decay factor. */
2387 long long proto_max_bulk_len; /* Protocol bulk length maximum size. */
2388 int oom_score_adj_values[CONFIG_OOM_COUNT]; /* Linux oom_score_adj configuration */
2389 int oom_score_adj; /* If true, oom_score_adj is managed */
2390 int disable_thp; /* If true, disable THP by syscall */
2391 /* Blocked clients */
2392 unsigned int blocked_clients; /* # of clients executing a blocking cmd.*/
2393 unsigned int blocked_clients_by_type[BLOCKED_NUM];
2394 list *unblocked_clients; /* list of clients to unblock before next loop */
2395 list *ready_keys; /* List of readyList structures for BLPOP & co */
2396 /* Client side caching. */
2397 unsigned int tracking_clients; /* # of clients with tracking enabled.*/
2398 size_t tracking_table_max_keys; /* Max number of keys in tracking table. */
2399 list *tracking_pending_keys; /* tracking invalidation keys pending to flush */
2400 list *pending_push_messages; /* pending publish or other push messages to flush */
2401 /* Sort parameters - qsort_r() is only available under BSD so we
2402 * have to take this state global, in order to pass it to sortCompare() */
2403 int sort_desc;
2404 int sort_alpha;
2405 int sort_bypattern;
2406 int sort_store;
2407 /* Zip structure config, see redis.conf for more information */
2408 size_t hash_max_listpack_entries;
2409 size_t hash_max_listpack_value;
2410 size_t set_max_intset_entries;
2411 size_t set_max_listpack_entries;
2412 size_t set_max_listpack_value;
2413 size_t zset_max_listpack_entries;
2414 size_t zset_max_listpack_value;
2415 size_t hll_sparse_max_bytes;
2416 size_t stream_node_max_bytes;
2417 long long stream_node_max_entries;
2418 /* Stream IDMP parameters */
2419 long long stream_idmp_duration; /* Default IDMP duration in seconds. */
2420 long long stream_idmp_maxsize; /* Default IDMP max entries. */
2421 /* List parameters */
2422 int list_max_listpack_size;
2423 int list_compress_depth;
2424 /* time cache */
2425 redisAtomic time_t unixtime; /* Unix time sampled every cron cycle. */
2426 time_t timezone; /* Cached timezone. As set by tzset(). */
2427 redisAtomic int daylight_active; /* Currently in daylight saving time. */
2428 mstime_t mstime; /* 'unixtime' in milliseconds. */
2429 ustime_t ustime; /* 'unixtime' in microseconds. */
2430 int accum_call_count_since_ustime; /* Command count since last ustime update */
2431 monotime monotonic_us_when_ustime; /* Monotonic time when last ustime update */
2432 mstime_t cmd_time_snapshot; /* Time snapshot of the root execution nesting. */
2433 size_t blocking_op_nesting; /* Nesting level of blocking operation, used to reset blocked_last_cron. */
2434 long long blocked_last_cron; /* Indicate the mstime of the last time we did cron jobs from a blocking operation */
2435 /* Pubsub */
2436 kvstore *pubsub_channels; /* Map channels to list of subscribed clients */
2437 dict *pubsub_patterns; /* A dict of pubsub_patterns */
2438 int notify_keyspace_events; /* Events to propagate via Pub/Sub. This is an
2439 xor of NOTIFY_... flags. */
2440 kvstore *pubsubshard_channels; /* Map shard channels in every slot to list of subscribed clients */
2441 unsigned int pubsub_clients; /* # of clients in Pub/Sub mode */
2442 unsigned int watching_clients; /* # of clients are wathcing keys */
2443 /* Cluster */
2444 int cluster_enabled; /* Is cluster enabled? */
2445 int cluster_port; /* Set the cluster port for a node. */
2446 mstime_t cluster_node_timeout; /* Cluster node timeout. */
2447 mstime_t cluster_ping_interval; /* A debug configuration for setting how often cluster nodes send ping messages. */
2448 char *cluster_configfile; /* Cluster auto-generated config file name. */
2449 long long asm_handoff_max_lag_bytes; /* Maximum lag in bytes before pausing writes for ASM handoff. */
2450 long long asm_write_pause_timeout; /* Timeout in milliseconds to pause writes during ASM handoff. */
2451 long long asm_sync_buffer_drain_timeout; /* Timeout in milliseconds for sync buffer to drain during ASM. */
2452 int asm_max_archived_tasks; /* Maximum number of archived ASM tasks to keep in memory. */
2453 struct clusterState *cluster; /* State of the cluster */
2454 int cluster_migration_barrier; /* Cluster replicas migration barrier. */
2455 int cluster_allow_replica_migration; /* Automatic replica migrations to orphaned masters and from empty masters */
2456 int cluster_slave_validity_factor; /* Slave max data age for failover. */
2457 int cluster_require_full_coverage; /* If true, put the cluster down if
2458 there is at least an uncovered slot.*/
2459 int cluster_slave_no_failover; /* Prevent slave from starting a failover
2460 if the master is in failure state. */
2461 char *cluster_announce_ip; /* IP address to announce on cluster bus. */
2462 char *cluster_announce_hostname; /* hostname to announce on cluster bus. */
2463 char *cluster_announce_human_nodename; /* Human readable node name assigned to a node. */
2464 int cluster_preferred_endpoint_type; /* Use the announced hostname when available. */
2465 int cluster_announce_port; /* base port to announce on cluster bus. */
2466 int cluster_announce_tls_port; /* TLS port to announce on cluster bus. */
2467 int cluster_announce_bus_port; /* bus port to announce on cluster bus. */
2468 int cluster_module_flags; /* Set of flags that Redis modules are able
2469 to set in order to suppress certain
2470 native Redis Cluster features. Check the
2471 REDISMODULE_CLUSTER_FLAG_*. */
2472 int cluster_module_trim_disablers; /* Number of module requests to disable trimming */
2473 int cluster_allow_reads_when_down; /* Are reads allowed when the cluster
2474 is down? */
2475 int cluster_config_file_lock_fd; /* cluster config fd, will be flocked. */
2476 unsigned long long cluster_link_msg_queue_limit_bytes; /* Memory usage limit on individual link msg queue */
2477 int cluster_drop_packet_filter; /* Debug config that allows tactically
2478 * dropping packets of a specific type */
2479 int cluster_slot_stats_enabled; /* Cluster slot usage statistics tracking enabled. */
2480 /* Scripting */
2481 unsigned int lua_arena; /* eval lua arena used in jemalloc. */
2482 mstime_t busy_reply_threshold; /* Script / module timeout in milliseconds */
2483 int pre_command_oom_state; /* OOM before command (script?) was started */
2484 int script_disable_deny_script; /* Allow running commands marked "noscript" inside a script. */
2485 int lua_enable_deprecated_api; /* Config to enable deprecated api */
2486 int key_memory_histograms; /* Config to enable key memory histograms */
2487 /* Lazy free */
2488 int lazyfree_lazy_eviction;
2489 int lazyfree_lazy_expire;
2490 int lazyfree_lazy_server_del;
2491 int lazyfree_lazy_user_del;
2492 int lazyfree_lazy_user_flush;
2493 /* Latency monitor */
2494 long long latency_monitor_threshold;
2495 dict *latency_events;
2496 /* ACLs */
2497 char *acl_filename; /* ACL Users file. NULL if not configured. */
2498 unsigned long acllog_max_len; /* Maximum length of the ACL LOG list. */
2499 sds requirepass; /* Remember the cleartext password set with
2500 the old "requirepass" directive for
2501 backward compatibility with Redis <= 5. */
2502 int acl_pubsub_default; /* Default ACL pub/sub channels flag */
2503 aclInfo acl_info; /* ACL info */
2504 /* Assert & bug reporting */
2505 int watchdog_period; /* Software watchdog period in ms. 0 = off */
2506 /* System hardware info */
2507 size_t system_memory_size; /* Total memory in system as reported by OS */
2508 /* TLS Configuration */
2509 int tls_cluster;
2510 int tls_replication;
2511 int tls_auth_clients;
2512 redisTLSContextConfig tls_ctx_config;
2513 /* cpu affinity */
2514 char *server_cpulist; /* cpu affinity list of redis server main/io thread. */
2515 char *bio_cpulist; /* cpu affinity list of bio thread. */
2516 char *aof_rewrite_cpulist; /* cpu affinity list of aof rewrite process. */
2517 char *bgsave_cpulist; /* cpu affinity list of bgsave process. */
2518 /* Sentinel config */
2519 struct sentinelConfig *sentinel_config; /* sentinel config to load at startup time. */
2520 /* Coordinate failover info */
2521 mstime_t failover_end_time; /* Deadline for failover command. */
2522 int force_failover; /* If true then failover will be forced at the
2523 * deadline, otherwise failover is aborted. */
2524 char *target_replica_host; /* Failover target host. If null during a
2525 * failover then any replica can be used. */
2526 int target_replica_port; /* Failover target port */
2527 int failover_state; /* Failover state */
2528 int cluster_allow_pubsubshard_when_down; /* Is pubsubshard allowed when the cluster
2529 is down, doesn't affect pubsub global. */
2530 long reply_buffer_peak_reset_time; /* The amount of time (in milliseconds) to wait between reply buffer peak resets */
2531 int reply_buffer_resizing_enabled; /* Is reply buffer resizing enabled (1 by default) */
2532 int reply_copy_avoidance_enabled; /* Is reply copy avoidance enabled (1 by default) */
2533 /* Local environment */
2534 char *locale_collate;
2535 unsigned int dbg_assert_flags; /* Bitmask of debug assertions to run after each command */
2536};
2537
2538/* Debug assertion flags for server.dbg_assert_flags */
2539#define DBG_ASSERT_KEYSIZES (1 << 0) /* Assert keysizes histogram */
2540#define DBG_ASSERT_ALLOC_SLOT (1 << 1) /* Assert per-slot alloc_size */
2541
2542/* we use 6 so that all getKeyResult fits a cacheline */
2543#define MAX_KEYS_BUFFER 6
2544
2545typedef struct {
2546 int pos; /* The position of the key within the client array */
2547 int flags; /* The flags associated with the key access, see
2548 CMD_KEY_* for more information */
2549} keyReference;
2550
2551/* A result structure for the various getkeys function calls. It lists the
2552 * keys as indices to the provided argv. This functionality is also re-used
2553 * for returning channel information.
2554 */
2555typedef struct {
2556 int numkeys; /* Number of key indices return */
2557 int size; /* Available array size */
2558 keyReference keysbuf[MAX_KEYS_BUFFER]; /* Pre-allocated buffer, to save heap allocations */
2559 keyReference *keys; /* Key indices array, points to keysbuf or heap */
2560} getKeysResult;
2561#define GETKEYS_RESULT_INIT { 0, MAX_KEYS_BUFFER, {{0}}, NULL }
2562
2563/*-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
2564 * Hotkey tracking
2565 *----------------------------------------------------------------------------*/
2566
2567/* Hotkeys tracking metric flags */
2568#define HOTKEYS_TRACK_CPU (1ULL << 0)
2569#define HOTKEYS_TRACK_NET (1ULL << 1)
2570#define HOTKEYS_METRICS_COUNT 2 /* NOTE: update if adding new metric */
2571
2572/* A structure for tracking hotkey statistics by given metrics. */
2573struct hotkeyStats {
2574 struct chkTopK *cpu;
2575 struct chkTopK *net;
2576 mstime_t start; /* Initial time point for wall time tracking */
2577
2578 /* Only keys from selected slots will be tracked. If slots is NULL,
2579 * all keys are tracked. Stored as a sorted slotRangeArray. */
2580 struct slotRangeArray *slots;
2581
2582 /* Statistics counters. */
2583 uint64_t time_sampled_commands_selected_slots; /* microseconds */
2584 uint64_t time_all_commands_selected_slots; /* microseconds */
2585 uint64_t time_all_commands_all_slots; /* microseconds */
2586 uint64_t net_bytes_sampled_commands_selected_slots;
2587 uint64_t net_bytes_all_commands_selected_slots;
2588 uint64_t net_bytes_all_commands_all_slots;
2589
2590 /* rusage stats for CPU time tracking */
2591 struct timeval ru_utime;
2592 struct timeval ru_stime;
2593
2594 int tracking_count; /* Count of top hotkeys we want to track */
2595 int sample_ratio; /* Track a key with probability 1 / sample_ratio */
2596 int active; /* True if tracking is currently active */
2597 mstime_t duration; /* Tracking duration */
2598 uint64_t tracked_metrics; /* Bit flags: HOTKEYS_TRACK_CPU, HOTKEYS_TRACK_NET, etc. */
2599 mstime_t cpu_time; /* Total CPU time spent updating the topk struct in milliseconds */
2600
2601 /* Current command related fields */
2602 getKeysResult keys_result; /* Key results for current command */
2603 client *current_client;
2604 int is_sampled; /* Indicates whether or not keys from cmd are sampled via sample_ratio */
2605 int is_in_selected_slots; /* Indicates whether or not keys from cmd are in selected_slots */
2606};
2607
2608typedef struct hotkeyMetrics {
2609 uint64_t cpu_time_usec;
2610 uint64_t net_bytes;
2611} hotkeyMetrics;
2612
2613/* pendingCommand flags */
2614enum {
2615 PENDING_CMD_FLAG_INCOMPLETE = 1 << 0, /* Command parsing is incomplete, still waiting for more data */
2616 PENDING_CMD_FLAG_PREPROCESSED = 1 << 1, /* This command has passed pre-processing */
2617 PENDING_CMD_KEYS_RESULT_VALID = 1 << 2, /* Command's keys_result is valid and cached */
2618};
2619
2620/* Parser state and parse result of a command from a client's input buffer. */
2621struct pendingCommand {
2622 int argc; /* Num of arguments of current command. */
2623 int argv_len; /* Size of argv array (may be more than argc) */
2624 robj **argv; /* Arguments of current command. */
2625 size_t argv_len_sum; /* Sum of lengths of objects in argv list. */
2626 unsigned long long input_bytes;
2627 struct redisCommand *cmd;
2628 getKeysResult keys_result;
2629 long long reploff; /* c->reploff should be set to this value when the command is processed */
2630 int flags;
2631 int slot; /* The slot the command is executing against. Set to INVALID_CLUSTER_SLOT
2632 * if no slot is being used or if the command has a cross slot error */
2633 uint8_t read_error;
2634
2635 struct pendingCommand *next;
2636 struct pendingCommand *prev;
2637};
2638
2639/* Key specs definitions.
2640 *
2641 * Brief: This is a scheme that tries to describe the location
2642 * of key arguments better than the old [first,last,step] scheme
2643 * which is limited and doesn't fit many commands.
2644 *
2645 * There are two steps:
2646 * 1. begin_search (BS): in which index should we start searching for keys?
2647 * 2. find_keys (FK): relative to the output of BS, how can we will which args are keys?
2648 *
2649 * There are two types of BS:
2650 * 1. index: key args start at a constant index
2651 * 2. keyword: key args start just after a specific keyword
2652 *
2653 * There are two kinds of FK:
2654 * 1. range: keys end at a specific index (or relative to the last argument)
2655 * 2. keynum: there's an arg that contains the number of key args somewhere before the keys themselves
2656 */
2657
2658/* WARNING! Must be synced with generate-command-code.py and RedisModuleKeySpecBeginSearchType */
2659typedef enum {
2660 KSPEC_BS_INVALID = 0, /* Must be 0 */
2661 KSPEC_BS_UNKNOWN,
2662 KSPEC_BS_INDEX,
2663 KSPEC_BS_KEYWORD
2664} kspec_bs_type;
2665
2666/* WARNING! Must be synced with generate-command-code.py and RedisModuleKeySpecFindKeysType */
2667typedef enum {
2668 KSPEC_FK_INVALID = 0, /* Must be 0 */
2669 KSPEC_FK_UNKNOWN,
2670 KSPEC_FK_RANGE,
2671 KSPEC_FK_KEYNUM
2672} kspec_fk_type;
2673
2674/* WARNING! This struct must match RedisModuleCommandKeySpec */
2675typedef struct {
2676 /* Declarative data */
2677 const char *notes;
2678 uint64_t flags;
2679 kspec_bs_type begin_search_type;
2680 union {
2681 struct {
2682 /* The index from which we start the search for keys */
2683 int pos;
2684 } index;
2685 struct {
2686 /* The keyword that indicates the beginning of key args */
2687 const char *keyword;
2688 /* An index in argv from which to start searching.
2689 * Can be negative, which means start search from the end, in reverse
2690 * (Example: -2 means to start in reverse from the penultimate arg) */
2691 int startfrom;
2692 } keyword;
2693 } bs;
2694 kspec_fk_type find_keys_type;
2695 union {
2696 /* NOTE: Indices in this struct are relative to the result of the begin_search step!
2697 * These are: range.lastkey, keynum.keynumidx, keynum.firstkey */
2698 struct {
2699 /* Index of the last key.
2700 * Can be negative, in which case it's not relative. -1 indicating till the last argument,
2701 * -2 one before the last and so on. */
2702 int lastkey;
2703 /* How many args should we skip after finding a key, in order to find the next one. */
2704 int keystep;
2705 /* If lastkey is -1, we use limit to stop the search by a factor. 0 and 1 mean no limit.
2706 * 2 means 1/2 of the remaining args, 3 means 1/3, and so on. */
2707 int limit;
2708 } range;
2709 struct {
2710 /* Index of the argument containing the number of keys to come */
2711 int keynumidx;
2712 /* Index of the fist key (Usually it's just after keynumidx, in
2713 * which case it should be set to keynumidx+1). */
2714 int firstkey;
2715 /* How many args should we skip after finding a key, in order to find the next one. */
2716 int keystep;
2717 } keynum;
2718 } fk;
2719} keySpec;
2720
2721#ifdef LOG_REQ_RES
2722
2723/* Must be synced with generate-command-code.py */
2724typedef enum {
2725 JSON_TYPE_STRING,
2726 JSON_TYPE_INTEGER,
2727 JSON_TYPE_BOOLEAN,
2728 JSON_TYPE_OBJECT,
2729 JSON_TYPE_ARRAY,
2730} jsonType;
2731
2732typedef struct jsonObjectElement {
2733 jsonType type;
2734 const char *key;
2735 union {
2736 const char *string;
2737 long long integer;
2738 int boolean;
2739 struct jsonObject *object;
2740 struct {
2741 struct jsonObject **objects;
2742 int length;
2743 } array;
2744 } value;
2745} jsonObjectElement;
2746
2747typedef struct jsonObject {
2748 struct jsonObjectElement *elements;
2749 int length;
2750} jsonObject;
2751
2752#endif
2753
2754/* WARNING! This struct must match RedisModuleCommandHistoryEntry */
2755typedef struct {
2756 const char *since;
2757 const char *changes;
2758} commandHistory;
2759
2760/* Must be synced with COMMAND_GROUP_STR and generate-command-code.py */
2761typedef enum {
2762 COMMAND_GROUP_GENERIC,
2763 COMMAND_GROUP_STRING,
2764 COMMAND_GROUP_LIST,
2765 COMMAND_GROUP_SET,
2766 COMMAND_GROUP_SORTED_SET,
2767 COMMAND_GROUP_HASH,
2768 COMMAND_GROUP_PUBSUB,
2769 COMMAND_GROUP_TRANSACTIONS,
2770 COMMAND_GROUP_CONNECTION,
2771 COMMAND_GROUP_SERVER,
2772 COMMAND_GROUP_SCRIPTING,
2773 COMMAND_GROUP_HYPERLOGLOG,
2774 COMMAND_GROUP_CLUSTER,
2775 COMMAND_GROUP_SENTINEL,
2776 COMMAND_GROUP_GEO,
2777 COMMAND_GROUP_STREAM,
2778 COMMAND_GROUP_BITMAP,
2779 COMMAND_GROUP_MODULE,
2780} redisCommandGroup;
2781
2782typedef void redisCommandProc(client *c);
2783typedef int redisGetKeysProc(struct redisCommand *cmd, robj **argv, int argc, getKeysResult *result);
2784
2785/* Redis command structure.
2786 *
2787 * Note that the command table is in commands.c and it is auto-generated.
2788 *
2789 * This is the meaning of the flags:
2790 *
2791 * CMD_WRITE: Write command (may modify the key space).
2792 *
2793 * CMD_READONLY: Commands just reading from keys without changing the content.
2794 * Note that commands that don't read from the keyspace such as
2795 * TIME, SELECT, INFO, administrative commands, and connection
2796 * or transaction related commands (multi, exec, discard, ...)
2797 * are not flagged as read-only commands, since they affect the
2798 * server or the connection in other ways.
2799 *
2800 * CMD_DENYOOM: May increase memory usage once called. Don't allow if out
2801 * of memory.
2802 *
2803 * CMD_ADMIN: Administrative command, like SAVE or SHUTDOWN.
2804 *
2805 * CMD_PUBSUB: Pub/Sub related command.
2806 *
2807 * CMD_NOSCRIPT: Command not allowed in scripts.
2808 *
2809 * CMD_BLOCKING: The command has the potential to block the client.
2810 *
2811 * CMD_LOADING: Allow the command while loading the database.
2812 *
2813 * CMD_NO_ASYNC_LOADING: Deny during async loading (when a replica uses diskless
2814 * sync swapdb, and allows access to the old dataset)
2815 *
2816 * CMD_STALE: Allow the command while a slave has stale data but is not
2817 * allowed to serve this data. Normally no command is accepted
2818 * in this condition but just a few.
2819 *
2820 * CMD_SKIP_MONITOR: Do not automatically propagate the command on MONITOR.
2821 *
2822 * CMD_SKIP_SLOWLOG: Do not automatically propagate the command to the slowlog.
2823 *
2824 * CMD_ASKING: Perform an implicit ASKING for this command, so the
2825 * command will be accepted in cluster mode if the slot is marked
2826 * as 'importing'.
2827 *
2828 * CMD_FAST: Fast command: O(1) or O(log(N)) command that should never
2829 * delay its execution as long as the kernel scheduler is giving
2830 * us time. Note that commands that may trigger a DEL as a side
2831 * effect (like SET) are not fast commands.
2832 *
2833 * CMD_NO_AUTH: Command doesn't require authentication
2834 *
2835 * CMD_MAY_REPLICATE: Command may produce replication traffic, but should be
2836 * allowed under circumstances where write commands are disallowed.
2837 * Examples include PUBLISH, which replicates pubsub messages,and
2838 * EVAL, which may execute write commands, which are replicated,
2839 * or may just execute read commands. A command can not be marked
2840 * both CMD_WRITE and CMD_MAY_REPLICATE
2841 *
2842 * CMD_SENTINEL: This command is present in sentinel mode.
2843 *
2844 * CMD_ONLY_SENTINEL: This command is present only when in sentinel mode.
2845 * And should be removed from redis.
2846 *
2847 * CMD_NO_MANDATORY_KEYS: This key arguments for this command are optional.
2848 *
2849 * CMD_NO_MULTI: The command is not allowed inside a transaction
2850 *
2851 * CMD_ALLOW_BUSY: The command can run while another command is running for
2852 * a long time (timedout script, module command that yields)
2853 *
2854 * CMD_TOUCHES_ARBITRARY_KEYS: The command may touch (and cause lazy-expire)
2855 * arbitrary key (i.e not provided in argv)
2856 *
2857 * CMD_INTERNAL: The command may perform operations without performing
2858 * validations such as ACL.
2859 *
2860 * The following additional flags are only used in order to put commands
2861 * in a specific ACL category. Commands can have multiple ACL categories.
2862 * See redis.conf for the exact meaning of each.
2863 *
2864 * @keyspace, @read, @write, @set, @sortedset, @list, @hash, @string, @bitmap,
2865 * @hyperloglog, @stream, @admin, @fast, @slow, @pubsub, @blocking, @dangerous,
2866 * @connection, @transaction, @scripting, @geo.
2867 *
2868 * Note that:
2869 *
2870 * 1) The read-only flag implies the @read ACL category.
2871 * 2) The write flag implies the @write ACL category.
2872 * 3) The fast flag implies the @fast ACL category.
2873 * 4) The admin flag implies the @admin and @dangerous ACL category.
2874 * 5) The pub-sub flag implies the @pubsub ACL category.
2875 * 6) The lack of fast flag implies the @slow ACL category.
2876 * 7) The non obvious "keyspace" category includes the commands
2877 * that interact with keys without having anything to do with
2878 * specific data structures, such as: DEL, RENAME, MOVE, SELECT,
2879 * TYPE, EXPIRE*, PEXPIRE*, TTL, PTTL, ...
2880 */
2881struct redisCommand {
2882 /* Declarative data */
2883 const char *declared_name; /* A string representing the command declared_name.
2884 * It is a const char * for native commands and SDS for module commands. */
2885 const char *summary; /* Summary of the command (optional). */
2886 const char *complexity; /* Complexity description (optional). */
2887 const char *since; /* Debut version of the command (optional). */
2888 int doc_flags; /* Flags for documentation (see CMD_DOC_*). */
2889 const char *replaced_by; /* In case the command is deprecated, this is the successor command. */
2890 const char *deprecated_since; /* In case the command is deprecated, when did it happen? */
2891 redisCommandGroup group; /* Command group */
2892 commandHistory *history; /* History of the command */
2893 int num_history;
2894 const char **tips; /* An array of strings that are meant to be tips for clients/proxies regarding this command */
2895 int num_tips;
2896 redisCommandProc *proc; /* Command implementation */
2897 int arity; /* Number of arguments, it is possible to use -N to say >= N */
2898 uint64_t flags; /* Command flags, see CMD_*. */
2899 uint64_t acl_categories; /* ACl categories, see ACL_CATEGORY_*. */
2900 keySpec *key_specs;
2901 int key_specs_num;
2902 /* Use a function to determine keys arguments in a command line.
2903 * Used for Redis Cluster redirect (may be NULL) */
2904 redisGetKeysProc *getkeys_proc;
2905 int num_args; /* Length of args array. */
2906 /* Array of subcommands (may be NULL) */
2907 struct redisCommand *subcommands;
2908 /* Array of arguments (may be NULL) */
2909 struct redisCommandArg *args;
2910#ifdef LOG_REQ_RES
2911 /* Reply schema */
2912 struct jsonObject *reply_schema;
2913#endif
2914
2915 /* Runtime populated data */
2916 long long microseconds, calls, rejected_calls, failed_calls;
2917 long long slowlog_count, slowlog_time_us_sum, slowlog_time_us_max;
2918 int id; /* Command ID. This is a progressive ID starting from 0 that
2919 is assigned at runtime, and is used in order to check
2920 ACLs. A connection is able to execute a given command if
2921 the user associated to the connection has this command
2922 bit set in the bitmap of allowed commands. */
2923 sds fullname; /* A SDS string representing the command fullname. */
2924 struct hdr_histogram* latency_histogram; /*points to the command latency command histogram (unit of time nanosecond) */
2925 keySpec legacy_range_key_spec; /* The legacy (first,last,step) key spec is
2926 * still maintained (if applicable) so that
2927 * we can still support the reply format of
2928 * COMMAND INFO and COMMAND GETKEYS */
2929 dict *subcommands_dict; /* A dictionary that holds the subcommands, the key is the subcommand sds name
2930 * (not the fullname), and the value is the redisCommand structure pointer. */
2931 struct redisCommand *parent;
2932 struct RedisModuleCommand *module_cmd; /* A pointer to the module command data (NULL if native command) */
2933};
2934
2935struct redisError {
2936 long long count;
2937};
2938
2939struct redisFunctionSym {
2940 char *name;
2941 unsigned long pointer;
2942};
2943
2944typedef struct _redisSortObject {
2945 robj *obj;
2946 union {
2947 double score;
2948 robj *cmpobj;
2949 } u;
2950} redisSortObject;
2951
2952typedef struct _redisSortOperation {
2953 int type;
2954 robj *pattern;
2955} redisSortOperation;
2956
2957/* Structure to hold list iteration abstraction. */
2958typedef struct {
2959 robj *subject;
2960 unsigned char encoding;
2961 unsigned char direction; /* Iteration direction */
2962
2963 unsigned char *lpi; /* listpack iterator */
2964 quicklistIter iter; /* quicklist iterator */
2965} listTypeIterator;
2966
2967/* Structure for an entry while iterating over a list. */
2968typedef struct {
2969 listTypeIterator *li;
2970 unsigned char *lpe; /* Entry in listpack */
2971 quicklistEntry entry; /* Entry in quicklist */
2972} listTypeEntry;
2973
2974/* Structure to hold set iteration abstraction. */
2975typedef struct {
2976 robj *subject;
2977 int encoding;
2978 int ii; /* intset iterator */
2979 dictIterator di;
2980 unsigned char *lpi; /* listpack iterator */
2981} setTypeIterator;
2982
2983/* Structure to hold hash iteration abstraction. Note that iteration over
2984 * hashes involves both fields and values. Because it is possible that
2985 * not both are required, store pointers in the iterator to avoid
2986 * unnecessary memory allocation for fields/values. */
2987typedef struct {
2988 robj *subject;
2989 int encoding;
2990
2991 unsigned char *fptr, *vptr, *tptr;
2992 uint64_t expire_time; /* Only used with OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX */
2993
2994 dictIterator di;
2995 dictEntry *de;
2996} hashTypeIterator;
2997
2998#include "stream.h" /* Stream data type header file. */
2999
3000#define OBJ_HASH_KEY 1
3001#define OBJ_HASH_VALUE 2
3002
3003/* Hash-field data type (of t_hash.c) - now using entry directly
3004 * Note: entry* is used directly instead of a typedef for clarity */

3479/* Return values for ACLCheckAllPerm(). */
3480#define ACL_OK 0
3481#define ACL_DENIED_CMD 1
3482#define ACL_DENIED_KEY 2
3483#define ACL_DENIED_AUTH 3 /* Only used for ACL LOG entries. */
3484#define ACL_DENIED_CHANNEL 4 /* Only used for pub/sub commands */
3485#define ACL_INVALID_TLS_CERT_AUTH 5 /* Only used for TLS Auto-authentication */
3486
3487/* Context values for addACLLogEntry(). */
3488#define ACL_LOG_CTX_TOPLEVEL 0
3489#define ACL_LOG_CTX_LUA 1
3490#define ACL_LOG_CTX_MULTI 2
3491#define ACL_LOG_CTX_MODULE 3
3492
3493/* ACL key permission types */
3494#define ACL_READ_PERMISSION (1<<0)
3495#define ACL_WRITE_PERMISSION (1<<1)
3496#define ACL_ALL_PERMISSION (ACL_READ_PERMISSION|ACL_WRITE_PERMISSION)
3497
3498/* Return codes for Authentication functions to indicate the result. */
3499typedef enum {
3500 AUTH_OK = 0,
3501 AUTH_ERR,
3502 AUTH_NOT_HANDLED,
3503 AUTH_BLOCKED
3504} AuthResult;

3537/* Sorted sets data type */
3538
3539/* Input flags. */
3540#define ZADD_IN_NONE 0
3541#define ZADD_IN_INCR (1<<0) /* Increment the score instead of setting it. */
3542#define ZADD_IN_NX (1<<1) /* Don't touch elements not already existing. */
3543#define ZADD_IN_XX (1<<2) /* Only touch elements already existing. */
3544#define ZADD_IN_GT (1<<3) /* Only update existing when new scores are higher. */
3545#define ZADD_IN_LT (1<<4) /* Only update existing when new scores are lower. */
3546
3547/* Output flags. */
3548#define ZADD_OUT_NOP (1<<0) /* Operation not performed because of conditionals.*/
3549#define ZADD_OUT_NAN (1<<1) /* Only touch elements already existing. */
3550#define ZADD_OUT_ADDED (1<<2) /* The element was new and was added. */
3551#define ZADD_OUT_UPDATED (1<<3) /* The element already existed, score updated. */
3552
3553/* Struct to hold an inclusive/exclusive range spec by score comparison. */
3554typedef struct {
3555 double min, max;
3556 int minex, maxex; /* are min or max exclusive? */
3557} zrangespec;
3558
3559/* Struct to hold an inclusive/exclusive range spec by lexicographic comparison. */
3560typedef struct {
3561 sds min, max; /* May be set to shared.(minstring|maxstring) */
3562 int minex, maxex; /* are min or max exclusive? */
3563} zlexrangespec;
3564
3565/* flags for incrCommandFailedCalls */
3566#define ERROR_COMMAND_REJECTED (1<<0) /* Indicate to update the command rejected stats */
3567#define ERROR_COMMAND_FAILED (1<<1) /* Indicate to update the command failed stats */

3589long zsetRank(robj *zobj, sds ele, int reverse, double *score);

3591robj *zsetDup(robj *o);

3727
3728/* Data structure for OBJ_ENCODING_LISTPACK_EX for hash. It contains listpack
3729 * and metadata fields for hash field expiration.*/
3730typedef struct listpackEx {
3731 ExpireMeta meta; /* To be used in order to register the hash in the
3732 global ebuckets subexpires with next, minimum,
3733 hash-field to expire. TTL value might be inaccurate
3734 up-to few seconds due to optimization consideration. */
3735 void *lp; /* listpack that contains 'key-value-ttl' tuples which
3736 are ordered by ttl. */
3737} listpackEx;
3738
3739/* Each dict of hash object that has fields with time-Expiration will have the
3740 * following metadata attached to dict header.
3741 * Note that alloc_size field must be first because hash objects without expre
3742 * already use sizeof(size_t) bytes of metadata for memory accounting. */
3743typedef struct htMetadataEx {
3744 size_t alloc_size; /* Total memory used for keys and values */
3745 ExpireMeta expireMeta; /* embedded ExpireMeta in dict.
3746 To be used in order to register the hash in the
3747 subexpires DB with next minimum hash-field to expire.
3748 TTL value might be inaccurate up-to few seconds due
3749 to optimization consideration. */
3750 ebuckets hfe; /* DS of Hash Fields Expiration, associated to each hash */
3751} htMetadataEx;
3752
3753/* hash metadata helpers */
3754static inline htMetadataEx *htGetMetadataEx(dict *d) {
3755 return (htMetadataEx *)dictMetadata(d);
3756}
3757
3758static inline size_t *htGetMetadataSize(dict *d) {
3759 return (size_t *)dictMetadata(d);
3760}
3761
3762/* Hash data type */
3763#define HASH_SET_TAKE_FIELD (1<<0)
3764#define HASH_SET_TAKE_VALUE (1<<1)
3765#define HASH_SET_COPY 0
3766
3767/* Hash field lazy expiration flags. Used by core hashTypeGetValue() and its callers */
3768#define HFE_LAZY_EXPIRE (0) /* Delete expired field, and if last field also the hash */
3769#define HFE_LAZY_AVOID_FIELD_DEL (1<<0) /* Avoid deleting expired field */
3770#define HFE_LAZY_AVOID_HASH_DEL (1<<1) /* Avoid deleting hash if the field is the last one */
3771#define HFE_LAZY_NO_NOTIFICATION (1<<2) /* Do not send notification, used when multiple fields
3772 * may expire and only one notification is desired. */
3773#define HFE_LAZY_NO_SIGNAL (1<<3) /* Do not send signal, used when multiple fields
3774 * may expire and only one signal is desired. */
3775#define HFE_LAZY_ACCESS_EXPIRED (1<<4) /* Avoid lazy expire and allow access to expired fields */
3776#define HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_KEYSIZES (1<<5) /* If field lazy deleted, avoid updating keysizes histogram */
3777#define HFE_LAZY_NO_UPDATE_ALLOCSIZES (1<<6) /* If field lazy deleted, avoid updating slot allocation sizes */
3778

3835/* Configuration */
3836/* Configuration Flags */
3837#define MODIFIABLE_CONFIG 0 /* This is the implied default for a standard
3838 * config, which is mutable. */
3839#define IMMUTABLE_CONFIG (1ULL<<0) /* Can this value only be set at startup? */
3840#define SENSITIVE_CONFIG (1ULL<<1) /* Does this value contain sensitive information */
3841#define DEBUG_CONFIG (1ULL<<2) /* Values that are useful for debugging. */
3842#define MULTI_ARG_CONFIG (1ULL<<3) /* This config receives multiple arguments. */
3843#define HIDDEN_CONFIG (1ULL<<4) /* This config is hidden in `config get <pattern>` (used for tests/debugging) */
3844#define PROTECTED_CONFIG (1ULL<<5) /* Becomes immutable if enable-protected-configs is enabled. */
3845#define DENY_LOADING_CONFIG (1ULL<<6) /* This config is forbidden during loading. */
3846#define ALIAS_CONFIG (1ULL<<7) /* For configs with multiple names, this flag is set on the alias. */
3847#define MODULE_CONFIG (1ULL<<8) /* This config is a module config */
3848#define VOLATILE_CONFIG (1ULL<<9) /* The config is a reference to the config data and not the config data itself (ex.
3849 * a file name containing more configuration like a tls key). In this case we want
3850 * to apply the configuration change even if the new config value is the same as
3851 * the old. */
3852
3853#define INTEGER_CONFIG 0 /* No flags means a simple integer configuration */
3854#define MEMORY_CONFIG (1<<0) /* Indicates if this value can be loaded as a memory value */
3855#define PERCENT_CONFIG (1<<1) /* Indicates if this value can be loaded as a percent (and stored as a negative int) */
3856#define OCTAL_CONFIG (1<<2) /* This value uses octal representation */
3857
3858/* Enum Configs contain an array of configEnum objects that match a string with an integer. */
3859typedef struct configEnum {
3860 char *name;
3861 int val;
3862} configEnum;
3863
3864/* Type of configuration. */
3865typedef enum {
3866 BOOL_CONFIG,
3867 NUMERIC_CONFIG,
3868 STRING_CONFIG,
3869 SDS_CONFIG,
3870 ENUM_CONFIG,
3871 SPECIAL_CONFIG,
3872} configType;
3873
3874void loadServerConfig(char *filename, char config_from_stdin, char *options);
3875void appendServerSaveParams(time_t seconds, int changes);
3876void resetServerSaveParams(void);
3877struct rewriteConfigState; /* Forward declaration to export API. */
3878int rewriteConfigRewriteLine(struct rewriteConfigState *state, const char *option, sds line, int force);
3879void rewriteConfigMarkAsProcessed(struct rewriteConfigState *state, const char *option);
3880int rewriteConfig(char *path, int force_write);
3881void initConfigValues(void);
3882void removeConfig(sds name);
3883sds getConfigDebugInfo(void);
3884int allowProtectedAction(int config, client *c);
3885void initServerClientMemUsageBuckets(void);
3886void freeServerClientMemUsageBuckets(void);
3887static inline int clusterSlotStatsEnabled(int stat) { return server.cluster_enabled && (server.cluster_slot_stats_enabled & stat); }
